title: Time resolution: calendars and slices
text: Annual averages hide makes energy systems hard: sun sets, wind stalls, demand peaks evening. model balances electricity year sees none — storage pointless, solar looks dispatchable, peak capacity free. calendar gives model sub-annual time slices, slice count main dial realism model size: Model variables scale roughly linearly slices — 96-slice UTOPIA base case solves seconds GLPK, 288-slice variant noticeably heavier.

title: Time resolution: calendars and slices
text: calendar’s structure nested named list: element level (e.g. SEASON, HOUR), holding slices. Slice names must alphanumeric (become set elements solver files). simplest form lists slice names — year divided equally: Unequal shares given per slice; nested list(<share>, <LEVEL> = ...) attaches child levels. UTOPIA’s 12-slice calendar makes peak hours short winter nights long:
code: tt <- make_timetable(list(   SEASON = c("WIN", "SPR", "SUM", "AUT"),   HOUR   = paste0("h", formatC(0:23, width = 2, flag = "0")) )) head(tt)          # 4 x 24 = 96 leaf slices, equal shares #>    ANNUAL SEASON   HOUR   slice      share weight #>    <char> <char> <char>  <char>      <num>  <num> #> 1: ANNUAL    AUT    h00 AUT_h00 0.01041667      1 #> 2: ANNUAL    AUT    h01 AUT_h01 0.01041667      1 #> 3: ANNUAL    AUT    h02 AUT_h02 0.01041667      1 #> 4: ANNUAL    AUT    h03 AUT_h03 0.01041667      1 #> 5: ANNUAL    AUT    h04 AUT_h04 0.01041667      1 #> 6: ANNUAL    AUT    h05 AUT_h05 0.01041667      1 tt12 <- make_timetable(list(   SEASON = list(     WIN = list(1 / 4, HOUR = list(DAY =  9 / 24, NGT = 12 / 24, PK = 3 / 24)),     SPR = list(1 / 4, HOUR = list(DAY = 11 / 24, NGT = 11 / 24, PK = 2 / 24)),     SUM = list(1 / 4, HOUR = list(DAY = 12 / 24, NGT =  9 / 24, PK = 3 / 24)),     AUT = list(1 / 4, HOUR = list(DAY = 11 / 24, NGT = 11 / 24, PK = 2 / 24))   ) )) head(tt12) #>    ANNUAL SEASON   HOUR   slice      share weight #>    <char> <char> <char>  <char>      <num>  <num> #> 1: ANNUAL    AUT    DAY AUT_DAY 0.11458333      1 #> 2: ANNUAL    AUT    NGT AUT_NGT 0.11458333      1 #> 3: ANNUAL    AUT     PK  AUT_PK 0.02083333      1 #> 4: ANNUAL    SPR    DAY SPR_DAY 0.11458333      1 #> 5: ANNUAL    SPR    NGT SPR_NGT 0.11458333      1 #> 6: ANNUAL    SPR     PK  SPR_PK 0.02083333      1

title: Time resolution: calendars and slices
text: newCalendar() turns timetable calendar object. Note name first argument — always pass timetable name (timetable =), name name/desc timetable lands right slot: derived slots bookkeeping model needs: @slice_share — slice’s share year (weight used whenever slice values aggregated); @timeframes — slice sets every level (ANNUAL, SEASON, …); @timeframe_rank — level hierarchy; commodity’s timeframe picks level balanced . autoplot() draws nested structure:
code: cal <- newCalendar(timetable = tt, name = "s4h24") cal@name #> [1] "s4h24" nrow(cal@slice_share)          # slices with their share of the year #> [1] 101 head(as.data.frame(cal@slice_share), 3) #>    slice share weight #> 1 ANNUAL  1.00      1 #> 2    AUT  0.25      1 #> 3    SPR  0.25      1 cal@timeframe_rank             # levels, coarsest (ANNUAL) to finest #> ANNUAL SEASON   HOUR  #>      1      2      3 autoplot(cal)

title: Time resolution: calendars and slices
text: package ships calendars list, built data-raw/calendars.R exactly grammar — including whole UTOPIA family: Pick one pass newModel(calendar = ...); UTOPIA vignettes use calendars$utopia_s4h24 throughout.
code: names(calendars) #> [1] "season_dn"                      "d365"                           #> [3] "utopia_annual"                  "utopia_seasons"                 #> [5] "utopia_s4h24"                   "utopia_m12h24"                  #> [7] "d365_h24"                       "d365_h24_subset_1day_per_month" calendars$utopia_seasons@desc #> [1] "UTOPIA: 4 seasons x 3 dayparts (DAY/NIGHT/PEAK), 12 slices"

title: Time resolution: calendars and slices
text: Slice names encode time; helpers translate encodings: tsl2dtm() / dtm2tsl() convert slice strings /date-times — useful joining model output observed hourly data.
code: hour2HOUR(c(0, 13, 23))        # hour of day -> "h00" "h13" "h23" #> [1] "h00" "h13" "h23" yday2YDAY(c(1, 365))           # day of year -> "d001" "d365" #> [1] "d001" "d365" head(tsl_formats)              # known slice-name formats (a dataset) #> [1] "d364"     "d365"     "d366"     "d364_h24" "d365_h24" "d366_h24"

title: Time resolution: calendars and slices
text: Every commodity carries timeframe — calendar level balanced . Fuels typically ANNUAL; electricity HOUR: process operates finest timeframe among commodities (gas plant producing hourly ELC dispatched hourly), overridable via newTechnology(timeframe = ...) — see model bricks article details. practical consequence: raising calendar’s resolution refines commodities processes whose timeframes follow — annual bookkeeping stays cheap.
code: ELC <- newCommodity("ELC", timeframe = "HOUR")    # balanced every slice COA <- newCommodity("COA", timeframe = "ANNUAL")  # balanced once a year

title: Time resolution: calendars and slices
text: Start coarse (utopia_seasons-like, ~12 slices) model structure flux — solves instant. Move hour-within-season (utopia_s4h24, 96) storage, VRE profiles peak pricing matter — intra-day dynamics need real hours. Full-year hourly detail (utopia_m12h24, 288 d365+hours) final runs; check tractability model_size() first. UTOPIA vignettes build one model run calendars interchangeably — resolution configuration choice, rewrite.

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: UTOPIA teaching model energy-system optimization – small Reference Energy System (RES) mapping primary resources conversion technologies electricity demand. vignette builds every block explicitly, one constructor call per object, can see exactly model assembled – ends packing blocks repository solvable model. companion vignette UTOPIA II: running scenarios loads ready-made kit ([utopia_modules], built steps) solves . inputs deterministic shipped package. Units. Capacity GW, energy/activity PJ, costs MEUR, emissions kt; cap2act = 31.536 (1 GW x 8760 h = 31.536 PJ). Cost figures rounded, catalogue-range values (Danish Energy Agency / NREL ATB): capex EUR/kW (= MEUR/GW via convert()), fixed O&M EUR/kW/yr, fuel EUR/GJ (= MEUR/PJ).
code: library(energyRt) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) have_sf <- requireNamespace("sf", quietly = TRUE)   # region maps need the sf package set_default_solver(solver_options$glpk)   # used by levcost() screening below

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: naming rules keep model portable readable. matter model exported several backends different case-sensitivity: GAMS case-insensitive, GLPK/MathProg, Python/Pyomo Julia/JuMP case-sensitive. model relies case distinguish names solve one backend break (silently merge names) another. Set elements UPPER-CASE. Every name appears set element – regions, commodities, technologies, groups, slices define – written capitals (R1, COA, ECOA, FUEL). rule guarantees identical behaviour across backends. Process names carry type prefix, role obvious glance names never collide across process families: Technologies conversion processes take fixed prefix – just upper-case mnemonic (ECOA = electricity coal, ESOL = electricity solar). build every object conventions – including inter-regional transmission (TBD_) rest--world fuel imports / electricity exports (IMP_/EXP_).

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: UTOPIA small multi-region country. package ships four 11-region map layouts utopia$map (squares, honeycomb, island, continent) — share region names R1…R11, model built one layout draws . Pick whichever suits story: vignette builds three-region model first three regions (R1, R2, R3) — draw transmission network honeycomb layout .
code: library(sf) op <- par(mfrow = c(2, 2), mar = c(0.5, 0.5, 2, 0.5)) for (nm in names(utopia$map)) {   m <- utopia$map[[nm]]   plot(st_geometry(m), col = hcl.colors(nrow(m), "Set 3"), border = "white", main = nm)   text(m$x, m$y, m$region, cex = 0.75) } par(op)

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: pick ready calendar – utopia_s4h24, four seasons x 24 hours (96 slices); calendar construction covered time-resolution article. Region names upper-case (R1, R2, R3), matching region names UTOPIA maps. Deterministic capacity-factor load profiles come [utopia_profiles()].
code: regions <- c("R1", "R2", "R3") cal  <- calendars$utopia_s4h24 prof <- utopia_profiles(regions, calendar = "utopia_s4h24") str(prof, max.level = 1)

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: Energy carriers, three renewable resource carriers (SOL/WIN/HYD) CO2 emission commodity. Fossil fuels carry emission factor (emis); biomass carbon-neutral. names upper-case set elements. emission intensities just declared, compared:
code: COA <- newCommodity(   name = "COA",   desc = "Steam coal",   timeframe = "ANNUAL",   emis = data.frame(comm = "CO2", unit = "kt/PJ", emis = 95) ) GAS <- newCommodity(   name = "GAS",   desc = "Natural gas",   timeframe = "ANNUAL",   emis = data.frame(comm = "CO2", unit = "kt/PJ", emis = 56) ) BIO <- newCommodity(   name = "BIO",   desc = "Solid biomass (carbon-neutral)",   timeframe = "ANNUAL" ) NUC <- newCommodity(   name = "NUC",   desc = "Nuclear fuel",   timeframe = "ANNUAL" ) SOL <- newCommodity(   name = "SOL",   desc = "Solar irradiation",   timeframe = "HOUR" ) WIN <- newCommodity(   name = "WIN",   desc = "Wind resource",   timeframe = "HOUR" ) HYD <- newCommodity(   name = "HYD",   desc = "Hydro inflow",   timeframe = "HOUR" ) ELC <- newCommodity(   name = "ELC",   desc = "Electricity",   timeframe = "HOUR" ) CO2 <- newCommodity(   name = "CO2",   desc = "Carbon dioxide emissions",   timeframe = "ANNUAL" ) autoplot(COA, GAS)

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: Fuel supplies (SUP_*) priced EUR/GJ (= MEUR/PJ); renewable resources (RES_*) free weather-limited. Endowments differ region – makes multi-region model interesting: R1 mines coal, R2 produces gas, R3 hydro. Regions without domestic fuel must import (next section) buy electricity grid – regional diversity stimulates trade. region column availability endowment lives. first two supplies full: remaining five differ name, commodity, price region set. family similar objects needed, wrapping constructor small helper idiomatic advanced pattern (packaged kit built exactly way):
code: SUP_COA <- newSupply(   name = "SUP_COA",   desc = "Coal supply (mined in R1)",   commodity = "COA",   unit = "PJ",   availability = data.frame(     region = "R1",                        # coal exists only in R1     cost = 2.5   ) ) SUP_GAS <- newSupply(   name = "SUP_GAS",   desc = "Natural gas supply (produced in R2)",   commodity = "GAS",   unit = "PJ",   availability = data.frame(     region = "R2",                        # gas exists only in R2     cost = 6.0   ) ) sup <- function(nm, comm, cost, reg = regions) newSupply(nm, commodity = comm,   availability = data.frame(region = reg, cost = cost))  SUP_BIO <- sup("SUP_BIO", "BIO", 8.0)              # biomass: everywhere SUP_NUC <- sup("SUP_NUC", "NUC", 0.9)              # nuclear fuel: world market RES_SOL <- sup("RES_SOL", "SOL", 0)                # sun shines everywhere... RES_WIN <- sup("RES_WIN", "WIN", 0)                # ...wind blows everywhere RES_HYD <- sup("RES_HYD", "HYD", 0, reg = "R3")    # hydro inflow: only R3

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: region can buy fuel rest world – premium domestic source. IMP_* objects add external supply price; EXP_* objects add external market buys price (capped, exports stay side business, objective): export price sits every technology’s levelized cost, model never builds capacity just export – sells genuine surplus. Two caps work together: exp.paces exports (annual 10 PJ spread slices via year-shares – bare exp.= 10 read 10 per slice), reserve bounds horizon total. Without pacing, model front-loads whole reserve base year, sunk fleet makes surplus cheapest.
code: IMP_COA <- newImport(   name = "IMP_COA",   desc = "Coal import from the rest of the world",   commodity = "COA",   unit = "PJ",   imp = data.frame(     region = regions,                     # available to every region     price = 3.5                           # vs 2.5 domestic in R1   ) ) IMP_GAS <- update(IMP_COA,   name = "IMP_GAS",   desc = "LNG import from the rest of the world",   commodity = "GAS",   imp = data.frame(     region = regions,     price = 9.0                           # vs 6.0 domestic in R2   ) )  EXP_ELC <- newExport(   name = "EXP_ELC",   desc = "Electricity export to the rest of the world",   commodity = "ELC",   unit = "PJ",   exp = merge(                            # ~10 PJ/yr per region, paced by slice     data.frame(region = regions, price = 5.0),     data.frame(slice  = as.data.frame(cal@slice_share)$slice,                exp.up = 10 * as.data.frame(cal@slice_share)$share)   ),   reserve = 300                           # plus a cumulative cap, PJ )

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: newWeather objects hold capacity factor per region slice solar, wind hydro – physical limits renewable technologies . Solar full, siblings via helper: autoplot() shows profile structure glance – solar day/season shape. Custom season names (WIN, SPR, …) defined calendar, pass resolve slice layout. Note regional endowments (utopia_profiles() scales profiles deterministically): R1 sunniest region, R2 windiest – one reason regions trade: (UTOPIA’s profiles derive IDEEA model India – note monsoon signature: summer solar minimum, spring maximum. Weather data, assumption.)
code: WSOL <- newWeather(   name = "WSOL",   desc = "Solar capacity factor",   timeframe = "HOUR",   weather = prof$weather[prof$weather$resource == "WSOL",                          c("region", "slice", "wval")] )  wobj <- function(res) newWeather(res, timeframe = "HOUR",   weather = prof$weather[prof$weather$resource == res,                          c("region", "slice", "wval")]) WWIN <- wobj("WWIN") WHYD <- wobj("WHYD") autoplot(WSOL, type = "line", calendar = cal)

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: Electricity demand follows deterministic load shape, weighted slice’s share year grown milestone years. Two views demand object: aggregated annual totals region (type = "area", default), within-year profiles region year (type = "line") – note morning ramp evening peak:
code: years  <- c(2020, 2030, 2040, 2050) growth <- c(1, 1.2, 1.4, 1.6) annual_demand <- 100                                   # base-year PJ per region  share <- as.data.frame(cal@slice_share)[, c("slice", "share")] d0 <- merge(prof$demand, share, by = "slice") d0$w <- d0$load * d0$share dem_rows <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(years), function(i) {   do.call(rbind, lapply(regions, function(r) {     dr <- d0[d0$region == r, ]     data.frame(region = r, year = years[i], slice = dr$slice,                dem = annual_demand * growth[i] * dr$w / sum(dr$w))   })) }))  DEM_ELC <- newDemand(   name = "DEM_ELC",   desc = "Final electricity demand",   commodity = "ELC",   unit = "PJ",   dem = dem_rows ) autoplot(DEM_ELC, years = 2020:2050) autoplot(DEM_ELC, type = "line", years = c(2020, 2050))

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: Thermal plants combust fuel (combustion = 1) convert electricity efficiency; renewables convert free resource capped weather. Two modeling choices run : Existing stock retires schedule. capacity$stock declared just base year declining path: energyRt interpolates anchors, base-year fleet fades naturally – faster solar wind (mid-life already), ~30 years thermal plants, longer nuclear, longest hydro. optimizeRetirement = TRUE additionally lets model retire capacity earlier saves money. Investment windows. start end bound years new capacity can built – coal phase-limited, nuclear needs lead time, hydro sites nearly exhausted. chart windows assembling model.

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
code: ECOA <- newTechnology(   name = "ECOA",   desc = "Coal power plant",   input = list(     comm = "COA",     unit = "PJ",     combustion = 1   ),   output = list(     comm = "ELC",     unit = "PJ"   ),   cap2act = 31.536,   ceff = data.frame(     comm = "COA",     cinp2use = 0.40                       # 40% efficiency   ),   invcost = list(     invcost = convert("EUR/kW", "MEUR/GW", 2000)   ),   fixom = list(     fixom = 55   ),   capacity = data.frame(                  # fleet clusters near the mines (R1)     region = c("R1", "R2", "R3", "R1", "R2", "R3"),     year   = c(2020, 2020, 2020, 2050, 2050, 2050),     stock  = c(   8,    3,    3,    0,    0,    0)   ),   start = list(start = 2010),   end   = list(end   = 2030),             # no new coal after 2030   olife = list(olife = 30),   optimizeRetirement = TRUE ) draw(ECOA)

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: gas plant structure – spell (last time; siblings use update()):
code: EGAS <- newTechnology(   name = "EGAS",   desc = "Gas power plant (CCGT)",   input = list(     comm = "GAS",     unit = "PJ",     combustion = 1   ),   output = list(     comm = "ELC",     unit = "PJ"   ),   cap2act = 31.536,   ceff = data.frame(     comm = "GAS",     cinp2use = 0.58   ),   invcost = list(     invcost = convert("EUR/kW", "MEUR/GW", 900)   ),   fixom = list(     fixom = 25   ),   capacity = data.frame(                  # gas plants sit on the gas (R2)     region = c("R1", "R2", "R3", "R1", "R2", "R3"),     year   = c(2020, 2020, 2020, 2050, 2050, 2050),     stock  = c(   1,    6,    1,    0,    0,    0)   ),   start = list(start = 2010),   olife = list(olife = 30),   optimizeRetirement = TRUE )

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: Nuclear must-run baseload (af.lo = 0.7), long-lived existing fleet (retiring 2060) build window opens 2025 (licensing construction lead times):
code: ENUC <- newTechnology(   name = "ENUC",   desc = "Nuclear power plant",   input = list(     comm = "NUC",     unit = "PJ"   ),   output = list(     comm = "ELC",     unit = "PJ"   ),   cap2act = 31.536,   ceff = data.frame(     comm = "NUC",     cinp2use = 0.35   ),   af = data.frame(     af.lo = 0.7                           # must-run baseload   ),   invcost = list(     invcost = convert("EUR/kW", "MEUR/GW", 8000)   # recent Western builds   ),   fixom = list(     fixom = 120   ),   capacity = data.frame(     region = c("R1", "R2", "R3", "R1", "R2", "R3"),     year   = c(2020, 2020, 2020, 2060, 2060, 2060),     stock  = c(   2,    2,    2,    0,    0,    0)   ),   start = list(start = 2025),   olife = list(olife = 50) )

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: VRE technologies draw free resource bounded weather capacity factor (waf.= 1 means output can reach profile, never exceed ). Solar, full: Wind hydro share solar’s structure, instead retyping derive update() – takes existing object replaces slots name. package’s intended way build families similar processes: Note EWIN inherited solar’s capacity path window untouched, EHYD overrides : hydro fleet exists R3 (12 GW, operating 2070s) investment window closed – model can run legacy dams never build new ones. since RES_HYD flows R3, regions can tap cheap resource transmission lines.
code: ESOL <- newTechnology(   name = "ESOL",   desc = "Utility-scale solar PV",   input = list(     comm = "SOL",     unit = "PJ"   ),   output = list(     comm = "ELC",     unit = "PJ"   ),   cap2act = 31.536,   ceff = data.frame(     comm = "SOL",     cinp2use = 1   ),   weather = list(     weather = "WSOL",     comm = "SOL",     waf.up = 1   ),   invcost = list(     invcost = convert("EUR/kW", "MEUR/GW", 650)   ),   fixom = list(     fixom = 12   ),   capacity = data.frame(                  # young but small fleet, out by 2040     region = c("R1", "R2", "R3", "R1", "R2", "R3"),     year   = c(2020, 2020, 2020, 2040, 2040, 2040),     stock  = c(   1,    1,    1,    0,    0,    0)   ),   start = list(start = 2015),   olife = list(olife = 25) ) EWIN <- update(ESOL,   name = "EWIN",   desc = "Onshore wind",   input = list(comm = "WIN", unit = "PJ"),   ceff = data.frame(comm = "WIN", cinp2use = 1),   weather = list(weather = "WWIN", comm = "WIN", waf.up = 1),   invcost = list(invcost = convert("EUR/kW", "MEUR/GW", 1300)),   fixom = list(fixom = 35) )  EHYD <- update(EWIN,   name = "EHYD",   desc = "Hydro power (only in R3, no new sites)",   input = list(comm = "HYD", unit = "PJ"),   ceff = data.frame(comm = "HYD", cinp2use = 1),   weather = list(weather = "WHYD", comm = "HYD", waf.up = 1),   invcost = list(invcost = convert("EUR/kW", "MEUR/GW", 3000)),   fixom = list(fixom = 45),   capacity = data.frame(                  # the hydro endowment sits in R3     region = c("R3", "R3"),     year   = c(2020, 2070),     stock  = c(  12,    0)   ),   start = list(start = 2010),   end   = list(end   = 2010),             # window closed: NO new hydro at all   olife = list(olife = 60) ) draw(EHYD)

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: EBIO burns biomass make electricity. Biomass carbon-neutral (BIO commodity carries emission factor), EBIO dispatchable zero-carbon generator – expensive fuel coal, emitting nothing: levelized cost (levcost() builds solves tiny single-technology model; annual commodities make LCOE read MEUR/PJ): Commodity groups (fuel blend input side, product split output side) covered structurally Model bricks article. left solvable UTOPIA model pending fix grouped technologies enforce input-output balance.
code: EBIO <- newTechnology(   name = "EBIO",   desc = "Biomass power plant (carbon-neutral)",   input = list(     comm = "BIO",     unit = "PJ",     combustion = 1   ),   output = list(     comm = "ELC",     unit = "PJ"   ),   ceff = data.frame(     comm = "BIO",     cinp2use = 0.35                       # 35% efficiency   ),   cap2act = 31.536,   invcost = list(     invcost = convert("EUR/kW", "MEUR/GW", 2200)   ),   fixom = list(     fixom = 60   ),   start = list(start = 2025),             # a new option, available from 2025   olife = list(olife = 30),   optimizeRetirement = TRUE ) draw(EBIO) ELCa <- newCommodity("ELCa", timeframe = "ANNUAL") EBIO_lc <- update(EBIO, output = list(comm = "ELCa")) lc <- levcost(EBIO_lc, discount = 0.05, verbose = FALSE,   repo = newRepository("r", BIO, CO2, ELCa),   fuel_costs = c(BIO = 8.0)) lc$levcost_npv autoplot(lc, type = "components")

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: 4-hour battery (STG_*) shifts electricity slices:
code: STG_ELC <- newStorage(   name = "STG_ELC",   desc = "Grid battery, 4-hour",   commodity = "ELC",   invcost = list(     invcost = convert("EUR/kWh", "MEUR/PJ", 200)   ),   cap2stg = 4,                            # 4 hours of storage per GW   seff = data.frame(     inpeff = 0.95,     outeff = 0.95                         # ~90% round trip   ),   olife = list(olife = 20) )

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: Trade objects open routes regions commodity. UTOPIA’s three regions form line, two bi-directional transmission links (TBD_) connect . newTrade lists routes (directions), transfer efficiency (teff – losses), endogenous capacity investment cost (capacityVariable = TRUE lets model expand line): draw() shows one link’s schematic; autoplot() (plot_trade_map()) puts whole network map — pass layout draw . Bidirectional links render double-headed arrows:
code: TBD_ELC_R1_R2 <- newTrade(   name = "TBD_ELC_R1_R2",   desc = "Bidirectional transmission line R1-R2",   commodity = "ELC",   routes = data.frame(     src = c("R1", "R2"),     dst = c("R2", "R1")   ),   trade = data.frame(     src = c("R1", "R2"),     dst = c("R2", "R1"),     teff = 0.97                           # 3% losses per transfer   ),   capacity = data.frame(     stock = 1                             # 1 GW existing interconnector   ),   capacityVariable = TRUE,                # the model may build more   invcost = data.frame(     region = c("R1", "R2"),     invcost = 350                         # MEUR/GW (per line end)   ),   olife = list(olife = 50) )  TBD_ELC_R2_R3 <- update(TBD_ELC_R1_R2,   name = "TBD_ELC_R2_R3",   desc = "Bidirectional transmission line R2-R3",   routes = data.frame(     src = c("R2", "R3"),     dst = c("R3", "R2")   ),   trade = data.frame(     src = c("R2", "R3"),     dst = c("R3", "R2"),     teff = 0.97   ),   invcost = data.frame(     region = c("R2", "R3"),     invcost = 350   ) ) draw(TBD_ELC_R1_R2) plot_trade_map(list(TBD_ELC_R1_R2, TBD_ELC_R2_R3), map = utopia$map$honeycomb)

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: Policies pathways pre-built objects layered onto base model running scenarios (mirroring IDEEA’s CO2_CAP etc.): CO2_CAP – declining cap CO2 emissions (newConstraint vEmsFuelTot; starts near BASE emissions (~5,000 kt/region 2020) tightens deep cut 2050); CT_CO2 – rising carbon tax (newTax; costs MEUR emissions kt, 20-80 EUR/t reads 0.02-0.08 MEUR/kt – watch units); RES_SHARE – growing renewable-generation floor; NO_NEW_NUC – new nuclear capacity; EARLY_RET – forced early retirement coal gas fleets (declining per-region ceiling installed coal gas capacity; optimizeRetirement = TRUE plants lets model choose units close).
code: nreg <- length(regions) CO2_CAP <- newConstraint(   name = "CO2_CAP",   eq = "<=",   for.each = data.frame(year = years, comm = "CO2"),   term1 = list(variable = "vEmsFuelTot"),   rhs = data.frame(year = range(years), rhs = c(5000, 2500) * nreg),   defVal = Inf )  CT_CO2 <- newTax(   name = "CT_CO2",   comm = "CO2",   tax = data.frame(year = range(years), bal = c(0.02, 0.08))  # 20-80 EUR/t )  ren <- c("ESOL", "EWIN", "EHYD") sh  <- seq(0.15, 0.50, length.out = length(years)) RES_SHARE <- newConstraint(   name = "RES_SHARE",   eq = ">=",   for.each = data.frame(year = years, comm = "ELC"),   term1 = list(variable = "vTechOut", for.sum = list(tech = ren)),   rhs = data.frame(year = years, rhs = sh * annual_demand * growth * nreg),   defVal = 0 )  NO_NEW_NUC <- newConstraint(   name = "NO_NEW_NUC",   eq = "<=",   for.each = data.frame(year = years, tech = "ENUC"),   term1 = list(variable = "vTechNewCap"),   rhs = 0,   defVal = 0 )  # vTechCap is indexed by region, so this ceiling applies region by region. # for.each is the full year x tech grid (expand.grid, not a recycled # data.frame); rhs sets a declining per-region ceiling. The binding effect is # on GAS -- without it the model builds gas out as the flexible VRE backstop; # coal already phases out on economics. A full fossil exit is infeasible here # (must-run nuclear + limited transmission need firm capacity for peak), so the # schedule keeps a backup floor. defVal = Inf leaves 2020 unbound. EARLY_RET <- newConstraint(   name = "EARLY_RET",   eq = "<=",   # scope to 2030+ only: adding the 2020 milestone (even unbound) makes the LP   # infeasible through rhs interpolation.   for.each = expand.grid(year = c(2030, 2040, 2050), tech = c("ECOA", "EGAS"),                          stringsAsFactors = FALSE),   term1 = list(variable = "vTechCap"),   rhs = data.frame(     tech = rep(c("ECOA", "EGAS"), each = 3),     year = rep(c(2030, 2040, 2050), 2),     rhs  = c(12, 10, 8,   7, 6, 5)     # coal & gas ceilings (GW/region), falling   ),   defVal = Inf )

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: Collect every block base repository, bind region set, calendar horizon get solvable model:
code: repo <- newRepository("utopia",   COA, GAS, BIO, NUC, SOL, WIN, HYD, ELC, CO2,          # commodities   SUP_COA, SUP_GAS, SUP_BIO, SUP_NUC,                   # fuel supplies   RES_SOL, RES_WIN, RES_HYD,                            # renewable resources   IMP_COA, IMP_GAS, EXP_ELC,                            # rest-of-world trade   WSOL, WWIN, WHYD,                                     # weather   ECOA, EGAS, ENUC, ESOL, EWIN, EHYD, EBIO,             # technologies   STG_ELC,                                              # storage   TBD_ELC_R1_R2, TBD_ELC_R2_R3,                         # interregional trade   DEM_ELC)                                              # demand length(repo); names(repo) hor <- newHorizon(period = 2020:2050, intervals = c(1, 10, 10, 10),                   mid_is_end = TRUE, name = "base") mod <- newModel("UTOPIA", data = repo, calendar = cal,                 region = regions, horizon = hor, discount = 0.05) mod

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: start/end choices define process can built; translucent tails show long last vintage operates (end + olife). One call charts whole fleet: complete UTOPIA model, ready interpolate_model() + solve_scenario() – subject UTOPIA II.
code: autoplot(mod, type = "windows")

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: model assembled can compare technologies solving. levcost(mod, name = ...) prices one technology building solving tiny unit-demand model inherits model’s regions, calendar, discount rate fuel supplies. default reports annual levelized cost: weather-driven technology’s profile collapsed annual capacity factor, capacity sized serve demand factor (textbook LCOE). -priori screening ranks technologies stand-alone cost. realized cost solved system – plant runs hours spreads fixed costs little output, variable renewable’s value depends produces. (timeframe = "native", ex-post cost solved scenario) see UTOPIA II.
code: techs <- c("ECOA", "EGAS", "ENUC", "ESOL", "EWIN", "EHYD") lcoe  <- sapply(techs, function(tn)   as.numeric(levcost(mod, name = tn, verbose = FALSE)$levcost_npv)) round(sort(lcoe), 1)                       # MEUR/PJ  (x3.6 ~ EUR/MWh) data.frame(tech = names(lcoe), lcoe = lcoe) |>   ggplot(aes(reorder(tech, lcoe), lcoe, fill = tech)) +   geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) + coord_flip() +   labs(x = NULL, y = "levelized cost, MEUR/PJ",        title = "A-priori technology cost (annual LCOE)") + theme_bw()

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: technology, whole model, (solving) scenario can rendered shareable HTML/PDF document – parameters, diagrams, cost breakdowns included. run (reports open browser); try session:
code: # one technology: datasheet with diagram, share frontier and levelized cost report(EBIO, discount = 0.05,        repo = newRepository("r", COA, BIO, CO2, ELCa),        fuel_costs = c(COA = 2.5, BIO = 8.0))  # a single process, priced inside the assembled model report(mod, name = "ENUC")  # the WHOLE model: configuration, inventory, every process one-by-one report(mod)  # a solved scenario: results overview (see UTOPIA II) # report(scen_BASE)

title: UTOPIA I: building the model
text: steps exactly data-raw/utopia_modules.R runs region layouts (1, 3 7 regions) produce shipped [utopia_modules] dataset – ready base repository ($repo), individual blocks, levers, alongside calendars, horizons maps: Continue UTOPIA II: running scenarios, loads kit, assembles model solves base case policy scenarios.
code: names(utopia_modules) names(utopia_modules$electricity)                 # kits by number of regions um <- utopia_modules$electricity$reg3 identical(names(repo), names(um$repo))            # same blocks we just built

title: UTOPIA II: running scenarios
text: vignette uses packaged [utopia_modules] kit (built UTOPIA : building model modules) assemble energy-system model run scenarios GLPK (bundled, external solver needed). Two calls run every scenario vignette: interpolate_model() expands model regions, years slices; solve_scenario() writes solver files, runs default solver (set ) reads solution. echo = FALSE keeps solver log page — drop watch GLPK work.
code: library(energyRt) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) have_sf <- requireNamespace("sf", quietly = TRUE)   # region maps need the sf package set_scenarios_path(file.path(tempdir(), "utopia")) set_default_solver(solver_options$glpk)             # set once, used by every solve

title: UTOPIA II: running scenarios
text: utopia_modules$electricity offers ready region layouts (reg1, reg3, reg7); kit base repository $repo scenario levers. Calendars, horizons maps sit alongside. (custom layout calendar, build blocks following UTOPIA : building model.)
code: names(utopia_modules$electricity)          # available layouts um  <- utopia_modules$electricity$reg3     # the 3-region base case cal <- utopia_modules$calendars$utopia_s4h24 hor <- utopia_modules$horizons$base        # milestones 2020/2030/2040/2050

what: Assembling and solving the base model
title: UTOPIA II: running scenarios
text: reg3 kit wires regions bi-directional transmission links (TBD_ELC_*). plot_trade_map() draws network map layout — pass utopia_modules$maps (honeycomb); 7-region kit shows six links: Electricity generation technology: dispatch representative summer day – generation stacked technology, storage charging zero, demand line (autoplot(scenario) reads solution via getMix(); note getData() aggregates sub-annual values ANNUAL default – pass timeframe = "highest" slice-level data):
code: mod <- newModel("UTOPIA", data = um$repo, calendar = cal,                 region = um$regions, horizon = hor, discount = 0.05) scen_BASE <- interpolate_model(mod, name = "BASE") |>   solve_scenario(echo = FALSE) getData(scen_BASE, "vObjective", merge = TRUE)$value    # total system cost, MEUR plot_trade_map(um$repo, map = utopia_modules$maps$honeycomb) getData(scen_BASE, "vTechOut", comm = "ELC", merge = TRUE) |>   group_by(year, tech) |> summarise(PJ = sum(value), .groups = "drop") |>   ggplot(aes(factor(year), PJ, fill = tech)) + geom_col() +   labs(x = "year", title = "Generation by technology (BASE)") + theme_bw() autoplot(scen_BASE, "generation", slice = "^SUM_")

title: UTOPIA II: running scenarios
text: scenario adds pre-built lever base model. pattern always interpolate_model(mod, name, LEVER) solve_scenario(): fifth lever, EARLY_RET, puts declining per-region ceiling installed coal gas capacity – plants carry optimizeRetirement = TRUE, model chooses units close first constraint sets pace. ceiling bites hardest gas (flexible backstop wind solar), pushing investment nuclear, biomass storage instead. Compare CO2 emissions across scenarios: Total system cost (objective) scenario: carbon tax CO2 cap cut emissions different mechanisms (price vs quantity); renewable target raises cost can even raise CO2 relative carbon price – useful contrast policy discussion.
code: scen_CO2CAP <- interpolate_model(mod, "CO2CAP", um$CO2_CAP) |>       # emissions cap   solve_scenario(echo = FALSE) scen_CTAX <- interpolate_model(mod, "CARBONTAX", um$CT_CO2) |>       # carbon tax   solve_scenario(echo = FALSE) scen_RES <- interpolate_model(mod, "RES_SHARE", um$RES_SHARE) |>     # renewable target   solve_scenario(echo = FALSE) scen_NUC <- interpolate_model(mod, "NO_NEW_NUC", um$NO_NEW_NUC) |>   # no new nuclear   solve_scenario(echo = FALSE) scen_ERET <- interpolate_model(mod, "EARLY_RET", um$EARLY_RET) |>    # early fossil retirement   solve_scenario(echo = FALSE) scl <- list(BASE = scen_BASE, CO2CAP = scen_CO2CAP,             CARBONTAX = scen_CTAX, RES_SHARE = scen_RES,             EARLY_RET = scen_ERET) lapply(scl, function(s) getData(s, "vEmsFuelTot", comm = "CO2", merge = TRUE)) |>   bind_rows() |>   group_by(scenario, year) |> summarise(ktCO2 = sum(value), .groups = "drop") |>   ggplot(aes(factor(year), ktCO2, fill = scenario)) +   geom_col(position = "dodge") +   labs(x = "year", title = "CO2 emissions by scenario") + theme_bw() sapply(scl, function(s) round(getData(s, "vObjective", merge = TRUE)$value[1]))

title: UTOPIA II: running scenarios
text: levcost() solved scenario returns ex-post levelized cost process: discounted sum costs (annualised investment vTechEac, fixed variable O&M, attributed fuel) divided output solution. reads actual results – mini-model – exact model’s time resolution, calendar caveat. ENUC runs base-year capacity, capital already sunk – annualised-investment term eac ~0 realized cost fixed O&M plus fuel; newly built plant add eac . cost per PJ also depends hard plant run: output spreads fixed costs, lowering levelized cost. Priced across scenarios, ENUC’s LCOE tracks much existing fleet dispatched policy mix changes: (levcost(scen, name) attributes fuel vTechInp, works single-fuel technologies ; plant grouped fuel input need group activity instead.) full technology datasheet ex-post cost embedded one call away (report() renders HTML/PDF; needs pandoc):
code: lc <- levcost(scen_BASE, name = "ENUC")      # the existing nuclear fleet lc$levcost_npv                               # NPV levelized cost, MEUR/PJ autoplot(lc, type = "components")            # eac / fixom / varom / supply, by year sapply(scl, function(s) {   lc <- suppressMessages(levcost(s, name = "ENUC"))   if (inherits(lc, "levcost")) round(as.numeric(lc$levcost_npv), 2) else NA_real_ }) report(scen_BASE, name = "ENUC", format = "html")

title: UTOPIA II: running scenarios
text: code runs different layout – e.g. single-region reg1 7-region reg7 (heavier). Just swap kit:
code: um7 <- utopia_modules$electricity$reg7 mod7 <- newModel("UTOPIA7", data = um7$repo, calendar = cal,                  region = um7$regions, horizon = hor, discount = 0.05) scen7 <- interpolate_model(mod7, "BASE7") |>   solve_scenario(echo = FALSE)

title: UTOPIA II: running scenarios
text: Extend UTOPIA inter-regional trade (using utopia_modules$maps), technologies introduced via scenarios (CCS, hydrogen, CHP), honeycomb multi-region layout. See workshop exercises step--step build.

title: UTOPIA: a teaching energy-system model with energyRt
text: UTOPIA classic teaching model energy-system optimization: small Reference Energy System (RES) maps primary resources → conversion technologies → energy carrier → final demand, finds least-cost way meet demand planning horizon. vignette rebuilds UTOPIA energyRt end end – commodities, supply, demand, technologies (including renewables weather, storage, blended-fuel plant), multi-region layout, solves GLPK (bundled, external solver needed) analyses results. inputs deterministic shipped package: sub-annual calendars (calendars), representative capacity-factor / load profiles (utopia_weather, utopia_demand, utopia_stock), expanded regions [utopia_profiles()]. Time-resolution construction covered companion article; simply pick ready calendar. Units. Capacity GW, energy/activity PJ, costs MEUR, emissions kt. capacity--activity factor cap2act = 31.536 converts 1 GW running full year 31.536 PJ (1 GW x 8760 h). Cost figures illustrative, rounded values range published technology catalogues (e.g. Danish Energy Agency / NREL ATB): overnight capital cost EUR/kW (= MEUR/GW), fixed O&M EUR/kW/yr, fuel prices EUR/GJ (= MEUR/PJ).
code: library(energyRt) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) # keep all scenario files in a temporary folder set_scenarios_path(file.path(tempdir(), "utopia")) # capital-cost helper: EUR/kW -> MEUR/GW (numerically 1:1) meur_gw <- function(eur_per_kw) convert("EUR/kW", "MEUR/GW", eur_per_kw)

title: UTOPIA: a teaching energy-system model with energyRt
text: use utopia_s4h24 – four seasons x 24 hours (96 slices). full diurnal detail lets storage cycle within day, 96 slices keep model fast. (utopia_m12h24, 288 slices, higher-resolution option.) planning horizon uses four milestone years (2020, 2030, 2040, 2050); base year single year, 10-year steps.
code: cal <- calendars$utopia_s4h24 cal                      # 4 seasons x 24 hours = 96 slices hor <- newHorizon(period = 2020:2050, intervals = c(1, 10, 10, 10),                   mid_is_end = TRUE) hor@intervals

title: UTOPIA: a teaching energy-system model with energyRt
text: UTOPIA imaginary country. base case use three regions; package ships example maps utopia$map (honeycomb, continent, island, …) larger multi-region layouts trade networks.
code: regs <- c("reg1", "reg2", "reg3") prof <- utopia_profiles(regs, calendar = "utopia_s4h24")   # weather/demand/stock

title: UTOPIA: a teaching energy-system model with energyRt
text: Energy carriers CO2 emission commodity. Fossil fuels carry emission factor (emis, kt CO2 per PJ); biomass treated carbon-neutral.
code: COA <- newCommodity("COA", timeframe = "ANNUAL",   emis = data.frame(comm = "CO2", unit = "kt/PJ", emis = 95)) GAS <- newCommodity("GAS", timeframe = "ANNUAL",   emis = data.frame(comm = "CO2", unit = "kt/PJ", emis = 56)) BIO <- newCommodity("BIO", timeframe = "ANNUAL")   # carbon-neutral NUC <- newCommodity("NUC", timeframe = "ANNUAL") SOL <- newCommodity("SOL", timeframe = "HOUR") WIN <- newCommodity("WIN", timeframe = "HOUR") HYD <- newCommodity("HYD", timeframe = "HOUR") ELC <- newCommodity("ELC", timeframe = "HOUR")     # electricity (hourly) CO2 <- newCommodity("CO2", timeframe = "ANNUAL")   # emissions accounting

title: UTOPIA: a teaching energy-system model with energyRt
text: Primary resources, priced MEUR per PJ. Solar/wind/hydro “resource” commodities free; availability limited weather ().
code: # fuel prices in EUR/GJ (= MEUR/PJ) SCOA <- newSupply("SCOA", commodity = "COA", availability = data.frame(region = regs, cost = 2.5)) SGAS <- newSupply("SGAS", commodity = "GAS", availability = data.frame(region = regs, cost = 6.0)) SBIO <- newSupply("SBIO", commodity = "BIO", availability = data.frame(region = regs, cost = 8.0)) SNUC <- newSupply("SNUC", commodity = "NUC", availability = data.frame(region = regs, cost = 0.9)) SSOL <- newSupply("SSOL", commodity = "SOL", availability = data.frame(region = regs, cost = 0)) SWIN <- newSupply("SWIN", commodity = "WIN", availability = data.frame(region = regs, cost = 0)) SHYD <- newSupply("SHYD", commodity = "HYD", availability = data.frame(region = regs, cost = 0))

title: UTOPIA: a teaching energy-system model with energyRt
text: Electricity demand follows deterministic load shape utopia_demand (relative shape slice), scaled annual level per region grown horizon. utopia_profiles() already expanded shape regions.
code: share <- as.data.frame(cal@slice_share)[, c("slice", "share")] d0 <- merge(prof$demand, share, by = "slice") d0$w <- d0$load * d0$share                       # energy weight per slice years <- c(2020, 2030, 2040, 2050); growth <- c(1, 1.2, 1.4, 1.6) dem_rows <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(years), function(i) {   do.call(rbind, lapply(regs, function(r) {     dr <- d0[d0$region == r, ]     data.frame(region = r, year = years[i], slice = dr$slice,                dem = 100 * growth[i] * dr$w / sum(dr$w))   # ~100 PJ/yr in 2020   })) })) DEM <- newDemand("DEM_ELC", commodity = "ELC", dem = dem_rows)

title: UTOPIA: a teaching energy-system model with energyRt
text: Solar, wind hydro availability come representative capacity-factor profiles (utopia_weather, sourced reanalysis data). weather object holds capacity factor wval per region slice; technology references cap output.
code: wobj <- function(res) newWeather(res, timeframe = "HOUR",   weather = prof$weather[prof$weather$resource == res, c("region", "slice", "wval")]) WSOL <- wobj("WSOL"); WWIN <- wobj("WWIN"); WHYD <- wobj("WHYD")  # a quick look at the solar day-shape (summer vs winter) prof$weather |>   dplyr::filter(resource == "WSOL", region == "reg1",                 grepl("^(WIN|SUM)_", slice)) |>   dplyr::mutate(season = sub("_.*", "", slice),                 hour = as.integer(sub(".*_h", "", slice))) |>   ggplot(aes(hour, wval, colour = season)) + geom_line() +   labs(title = "Solar capacity factor by hour", y = "capacity factor") +   theme_bw()

title: UTOPIA: a teaching energy-system model with energyRt
text: Coal gas plants combust fuel (combustion = 1, CO2 counted) convert electricity given efficiency (ceff$cinp2use). Existing base-year capacity comes utopia_stock.
code: stk <- function(tech) {   g <- prof$stock[prof$stock$tech == tech, ]   data.frame(region = g$region, year = 2020, stock = g$gw) } thermal <- function(nm, comm, eff, inv, fixom) newTechnology(nm,   input = list(comm = comm, combustion = 1), output = list(comm = "ELC"),   ceff = data.frame(comm = comm, cinp2use = eff), cap2act = 31.536, fixom = fixom,   invcost = data.frame(region = regs, invcost = inv),   olife = 30L, start = 2010L, capacity = stk(nm), optimizeRetirement = TRUE)  ECOA <- thermal("ECOA", "COA", 0.40, meur_gw(2000), 55)   # coal EGAS <- thermal("EGAS", "GAS", 0.58, meur_gw(900), 25)    # CCGT ENUC <- newTechnology("ENUC", input = list(comm = "NUC"), output = list(comm = "ELC"),   ceff = data.frame(comm = "NUC", cinp2use = 0.35), af = data.frame(af.lo = 0.7),   cap2act = 31.536, fixom = 120, invcost = data.frame(region = regs, invcost = meur_gw(6500)),   olife = 50L, start = 2010L, capacity = stk("ENUC"))

title: UTOPIA: a teaching energy-system model with energyRt
text: Solar, wind hydro convert free resource electricity, output capped linked weather capacity factor.
code: vre <- function(nm, comm, wname, inv, fixom) newTechnology(nm,   input = list(comm = comm), output = list(comm = "ELC"),   ceff = data.frame(comm = comm, cinp2use = 1),   weather = list(weather = wname, comm = comm, waf.up = 1),   cap2act = 31.536, fixom = fixom, invcost = data.frame(region = regs, invcost = inv),   olife = 25L, start = 2015L, capacity = stk(nm)) ESOL <- vre("ESOL", "SOL", "WSOL", meur_gw(650), 12)     # utility solar PV EWIN <- vre("EWIN", "WIN", "WWIN", meur_gw(1300), 35)    # onshore wind EHYD <- vre("EHYD", "HYD", "WHYD", meur_gw(3000), 45)    # hydro

title: UTOPIA: a teaching energy-system model with energyRt
text: ECOABIO coal-biomass co-firing plant: burns blend two fuels. fuels sit one input group FUEL; group converts electricity (geff$ginp2use), fuel contributes group total (ceff$cinp2ginp), biomass share bounded (ceff$share.– 30% ). commodity-group / share machinery nutshell. feasible blend band requires solve – tech_share_frontier() intersects fuel’s share bounds: levelized cost look blend plant isolation. levcost() builds solves tiny single-technology model; using annual commodities makes LCOE read directly MEUR per PJ.
code: ECOABIO <- newTechnology("ECOABIO", desc = "Coal-biomass co-firing (blended fuel)",   input  = data.frame(comm = c("COA", "BIO"), group = "FUEL", combustion = c(1, 0)),   output = list(comm = "ELC"),   group  = data.frame(group = "FUEL", desc = "Blended solid fuel", unit = "PJ"),   geff   = data.frame(group = "FUEL", ginp2use = 0.40),   ceff   = data.frame(comm = c("COA", "BIO"), cinp2ginp = c(1, 1),                       share.up = c(1.0, 0.30)),   cap2act = 31.536, fixom = 60, invcost = data.frame(region = regs, invcost = meur_gw(2200)),   olife = 30L, start = 2010L, optimizeRetirement = TRUE) tech_share_frontier(ECOABIO)          # feasible coal/biomass share band ELCa <- newCommodity("ELCa", timeframe = "ANNUAL") ECOABIO_lc <- newTechnology("ECOABIO", desc = "Coal-biomass co-firing",   input  = data.frame(comm = c("COA", "BIO"), group = "FUEL", combustion = c(1, 0)),   output = list(comm = "ELCa"),   group  = data.frame(group = "FUEL", desc = "Blended solid fuel", unit = "PJ"),   geff   = data.frame(group = "FUEL", ginp2use = 0.40),   ceff   = data.frame(comm = c("COA", "BIO"), cinp2ginp = c(1, 1), share.up = c(1.0, 0.30)),   cap2act = 31.536, fixom = 60, invcost = list(invcost = meur_gw(2200)), olife = 30L) lc <- levcost(ECOABIO_lc, discount = 0.05, verbose = FALSE,   repo = newRepository("r", COA, BIO, CO2, ELCa), fuel_costs = c(COA = 2.5, BIO = 8.0)) lc$levcost_npv                        # NPV levelized cost, MEUR/PJ autoplot(lc, type = "components")     # cost breakdown by year # a full technology datasheet (schematic, tables, share frontier, LCOE): report(ECOABIO_lc, format = "html", discount = 0.05,        repo = newRepository("r", COA, BIO, CO2, ELCa))

title: UTOPIA: a teaching energy-system model with energyRt
text: battery shifts electricity slices – essential resolve 24-hour day, solar can serve evening demand.
code: # 4-hour battery; energy-capacity cost ~200 EUR/kWh STG <- newStorage("STGELC", commodity = "ELC", olife = 20L,   invcost = data.frame(region = regs, invcost = convert("EUR/kWh", "MEUR/PJ", 200)),   cap2stg = 4, seff = data.frame(inpeff = 0.95, outeff = 0.95))

what: Assemble and solve the base scenario
title: UTOPIA: a teaching energy-system model with energyRt
text: Interpolate model milestone years, solve scenario GLPK. Building scenario two steps – interpolate_model() solve_scenario() – pattern reuse every scenario .
code: repo <- newRepository("utopia",   COA, GAS, BIO, NUC, SOL, WIN, HYD, ELC, CO2,   SCOA, SGAS, SBIO, SNUC, SSOL, SWIN, SHYD, WSOL, WWIN, WHYD,   ECOA, EGAS, ENUC, ESOL, EWIN, EHYD, ECOABIO, STG, DEM) mod <- newModel("UTOPIA", data = repo, calendar = cal, region = regs,                 horizon = hor, discount = 0.05) scen_BASE <- solve_scenario(   interpolate_model(mod, name = "BASE"), solver = solver_options$glpk) getData(scen_BASE, "vObjective", merge = TRUE)$value   # total system cost, MEUR

title: UTOPIA: a teaching energy-system model with energyRt
text: Electricity generation technology year: Storage cycling within representative day (charges midday, discharges evening): CO2 emissions horizon:
code: gen <- getData(scen_BASE, "vTechOut", comm = "ELC", merge = TRUE) gen |>   group_by(year, tech) |> summarise(PJ = sum(value), .groups = "drop") |>   ggplot(aes(factor(year), PJ, fill = tech)) +   geom_col() + labs(x = "year", title = "Electricity generation by technology") +   theme_bw() getData(scen_BASE, "vStorageInp", merge = TRUE) |>   filter(year == 2050, grepl("^SUM_", slice)) |>   mutate(hour = as.integer(sub(".*_h", "", slice))) |>   group_by(hour) |> summarise(charge = sum(value), .groups = "drop") |>   ggplot(aes(hour, charge)) + geom_col() +   labs(title = "Battery charging, summer day (2050)", y = "PJ") + theme_bw() getData(scen_BASE, "vEmsFuelTot", comm = "CO2", merge = TRUE) |>   group_by(year) |> summarise(ktCO2 = sum(value), .groups = "drop")

title: UTOPIA: a teaching energy-system model with energyRt
text: Scenarios reuse base model add modify objects. contrast CO2 cap (quantity limit) carbon tax (price).

title: UTOPIA: a teaching energy-system model with energyRt
text: declining cap total CO2 emissions, tightening 40% base level:
code: base_emis <- getData(scen_BASE, "vEmsFuelTot", comm = "CO2", merge = TRUE) |>   group_by(year) |> summarise(v = sum(value), .groups = "drop") cap0 <- base_emis$v[base_emis$year == 2020] CO2CAP <- newConstraint(name = "CO2CAP", eq = "<=",   for.each = data.frame(year = years, comm = "CO2"),   term1 = list(variable = "vEmsFuelTot"),   rhs = data.frame(year = c(2020, 2050), rhs = round(cap0) * c(1.0, 0.4)),   defVal = Inf) scen_CO2CAP <- solve_scenario(   interpolate_model(mod, name = "CO2CAP", CO2CAP), solver = solver_options$glpk)

title: UTOPIA: a teaching energy-system model with energyRt
text: rising tax CO2 emissions: Compare emissions across scenarios:
code: CTAX <- newTax(name = "CTAX", comm = "CO2",   tax = data.frame(year = c(2020, 2050), bal = c(20, 80))) scen_CTAX <- solve_scenario(   interpolate_model(mod, name = "CARBONTAX", CTAX), solver = solver_options$glpk) lapply(list(BASE = scen_BASE, CO2CAP = scen_CO2CAP, CTAX = scen_CTAX),   function(s) getData(s, "vEmsFuelTot", comm = "CO2", merge = TRUE)) |>   bind_rows() |>   group_by(scenario, year) |> summarise(ktCO2 = sum(value), .groups = "drop") |>   ggplot(aes(factor(year), ktCO2, fill = scenario)) +   geom_col(position = "dodge") + labs(x = "year", title = "CO2 emissions by scenario") +   theme_bw()

title: UTOPIA: a teaching energy-system model with energyRt
text: base UTOPIA can extended inter-regional trade, additional technologies introduced via scenarios (CCS, hydrogen, CHP), renewable-share targets, nuclear phase-– small addition repository. See companion articles time-resolution construction workshop exercises step--step build.

title: Workflow: working with results and scenarios
text: getObject() returns building-block objects held repository, model scenario, filtered class, name, description, region object slot. object-level counterpart getData() (returns data). default returns named list keyed object name; drop = TRUE unwraps single match object . Region matching reads @region slots region/src/dst columns data.frame slot, works every class; region-agnostic objects (e.g. commodities) match every region. getObject() accepts scenario , query works solving.
code: repo <- um$repo names(getObject(repo, class = "technology"))        # all technologies #> [1] "ECOA" "EGAS" "ENUC" "ESOL" "EWIN" "EHYD" "EBIO" names(getObject(repo, class = "supply", region = "R1")) #> [1] "SUP_COA" "SUP_BIO" "SUP_NUC" "RES_SOL" "RES_WIN" getObject(repo, name = "ECOA", drop = TRUE)@invcost # the ECOA object itself #>   region year invcost wacc eac retcost #> 1   <NA>   NA    2000   NA  NA      NA

title: Workflow: working with results and scenarios
text: getData() pulls parameter variable data scenario tidy data.frames. merge = TRUE returns one long frame (named list otherwise); every frame carries scenario name column. ... accept set filters, exact (comm = "ELC") regex (comm_ = "^EL"). timeframe = c("lowest","highest","") controls slice-indexed values returned. Rename dimensions recode values way newNames = / newValues = (afterwards renameSets() / revalueSets()), discover parameters carry given set findData(): find_in_model(mod, "ECOA") text-searches every object slot value — handy locating name used.
code: getData(scen, "vObjective", merge = TRUE)$value      # total system cost, MEUR #> [1] 13007.08  gen <- getData(scen, "vTechOut", comm = "ELC", merge = TRUE) head(gen[, c("scenario", "tech", "region", "year", "slice", "value")], 4) #> # A tibble: 4 × 6 #>   scenario tech  region  year slice  value #>   <chr>    <chr> <chr>  <int> <chr>  <dbl> #> 1 BASE     ECOA  R1      2020 ANNUAL 21.7  #> 2 BASE     ECOA  R1      2030 ANNUAL 34.7  #> 3 BASE     ECOA  R1      2040 ANNUAL 25.5  #> 4 BASE     EGAS  R1      2040 ANNUAL  2.82 names(findData(scen, setsNames_ = "tech"))           # parameters indexed by 'tech' #>  [1] "pTechWeatherAf"       "pTechWeatherAfs"      "pTechWeatherAfc"      #>  [4] "pTechCap2act"         "pTechEac"             "pTechEmisComm"        #>  [7] "pTechOlife"           "pTechFixom"           "pTechInvcost"         #> [10] "pTechStock"           "pTechVarom"           "pTechAf"              #> [13] "pTechRampUp"          "pTechRampDown"        "pTechAfs"             #> [16] "pTechGinp2use"        "pTechCinp2ginp"       "pTechUse2cact"        #> [19] "pTechAct2AInp"        "pTechCap2AInp"        "pTechAct2AOut"        #> [22] "pTechCap2AOut"        "pTechNCap2AInp"       "pTechNCap2AOut"       #> [25] "pTechCinp2AInp"       "pTechCout2AInp"       "pTechCinp2AOut"       #> [28] "pTechCout2AOut"       "pTechCact2cout"       "pTechCinp2use"        #> [31] "pTechCvarom"          "pTechAvarom"          "pTechShare"           #> [34] "pTechAfc"             "pTechCap"             "pTechNewCap"          #> [37] "pTechRet"             "pTechRetCost"         "vTechInv"             #> [40] "vTechEac"             "vTechRetCost"         "vTechFixom"           #> [43] "vTechVarom"           "vTechNewCap"          "vTechRetiredStockCum" #> [46] "vTechRetiredStock"    "vTechRetiredNewCap"   "vTechCap"             #> [49] "vTechAct"             "vTechInp"             "vTechOut"             #> [52] "vTechAInp"            "vTechAOut"            "vTechEmsFuel"

title: Workflow: working with results and scenarios
text: update() edits slots single model object (technology, commodity, demand, …). operate whole model scenario — update object, put back repository add(..., overwrite = TRUE), re-interpolate/solve. (already-interpolated scenario, update_parameter(scen, param, data) writes rows straight parameter store.)
code: ECOA <- getObject(repo, name = "ECOA", drop = TRUE) ECOA <- update(ECOA, invcost = data.frame(invcost = 2500))  # pricier coal capex repo_hi <- add(repo, ECOA, overwrite = TRUE)                # swap it back in  mod_hi  <- newModel("UTOPIA_HI", data = repo_hi,                     calendar = utopia_modules$calendars$utopia_s4h24,                     region = um$regions, horizon = utopia_modules$horizons$base,                     discount = 0.05)

title: Workflow: working with results and scenarios
text: scenarios directory option — read get_scenarios_path(), set set_scenarios_path() (default "scenarios/"). scenario gets folder, named make_scenario_dirname() name_model_calendar_horizon: Saving scenario writes Arrow-backed folder mirrors scenario’s structure — thin scen.RData shell plus large data slot dataset: scenario knows whether data RAM disk via isInMemory(); disk, slots empty read lazily folder demand.
code: get_scenarios_path() #> [1] "C:\\Users\\admin\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\RtmpMPAFa0/wf" make_scenario_dirname(scen) #> [1] "BASE_UTOPIA_utopia_s4h24_base" <scenarios-path>/<name_model_calendar_horizon>/ ├── scen.RData          # thinned S4 scenario shell ├── class · format      # "scenario" · "parquet" ├── logfile.csv         # timestamped save log ├── model/data/<repo>/<object>/<slot>/   # the model objects' data ├── modInp/parameters/<param>/           # interpolated input parameters ├── modOut/variables/<var>/              # solved variables (vTechOut, ...) └── script/<backend>_<solver>_<method>/  # solver working directory

title: Workflow: working with results and scenarios
text: save_scenario() spills scenario folder returns (now -disk) object; load_scenario(path, env = NULL) reads shell back. data stays disk requested — getData() reads lazily, obj2mem() pulls whole scenario back memory. name-based scenario registry (register(), getScenario(), get_registry()) development; now, organize runs set_scenarios_path() round-trip save_scenario() / load_scenario().
code: saved <- save_scenario(scen, verbose = FALSE) ld    <- load_scenario(saved@path, env = NULL, verbose = FALSE) basename(saved@path)                                 # the scenario folder #> [1] "BASE_UTOPIA_utopia-s4h24_base" isInMemory(saved)                                    # FALSE -- data is on disk #> [1] FALSE getData(ld, "vObjective", merge = TRUE)$value        # lazy read, no full load #> [1] 13007.08 ld <- obj2mem(ld)                                    # rehydrate fully into RAM isInMemory(ld) #> [1] TRUE

title: Workflow: working with results and scenarios
text: Layer policy lever onto base model get second scenario, pass named list scenarios getData() — scenario column makes comparison one-liner:  reason model size rather results, model_size() estimates parameter/variable/constraint counts interpolated scenario, size() reports -memory footprint: systematic check build correct efficient, compare_interp_settings(mod) compare_solve_settings(mod, solvers = ...) interpolate (solve) model different storage settings tabulate build size, time — crucially — confirm objective invariant across fold/sparse/prune. run several builds, best run interactively:
code: scen_cap <- interpolate_model(mod, "CO2CAP", um$CO2_CAP) |>   # add the CO2 cap lever   solve_scenario(solver = solver_options$glpk, echo = FALSE) emis <- getData(list(BASE = scen, CO2CAP = scen_cap), "vEmsFuelTot",                 comm = "CO2", merge = TRUE)  emis |>   group_by(scenario, year) |> summarise(ktCO2 = sum(value), .groups = "drop") |>   ggplot(aes(factor(year), ktCO2, fill = scenario)) +   geom_col(position = "dodge") +   labs(x = "year", title = "CO2 emissions by scenario") + theme_bw() sapply(list(BASE = scen, CO2CAP = scen_cap),        function(s) round(getData(s, "vObjective", merge = TRUE)$value[1])) #>   BASE CO2CAP  #>  13007  13427 model_size(scen) #> model_size: BASE #>   parameters : 122 value, 236 maps, 13 sets #>   param rows : 5,938 #>   estimate   : ~15,338 variables, ~16,832 constraints (from gating maps) #>   top parameters by rows: #>     pTechCinp2use      1,536 #>     pWeather           1,004 #>     pSliceWeight       404 #>     pExportRowPrice    404 #>     pExportRow         404 #>     pSliceAgg          400 #>     pDemand            384 #>     pTechAf            384 #>     pStorageInpEff     384 #>     pStorageOutEff     384 #>     pSliceShare        101 #>     pTechFixom         23 #>     pTechEac           20 #>     pTechInvcost       20 #>     pTechStock         14 size(scen) #> [1] "6 Mb" compare_interp_settings(mod)                          # size/time by setting compare_solve_settings(mod, solvers = list(solver_options$glpk))

title: Workflow: working with results and scenarios
text: Model bricks UTOPIA — building objects model. Solver backends UTOPIA II — interpolating solving. ?getData, ?getObject, ?update, ?save_scenario, ?load_scenario, ?model_size, ?compare_solve_settings.

Unit 'use' (grouped input) applicable. activity character. Unit activity variable technology. costs character. Currency costs variable technology. group data.frame. Input-commodity-group efficiency parameters. region character. Name region apply parameter, NA every region. year integer. Year apply parameter, NA every year. slice character. title: Generate an R script that recreates an energyRt object — design
text: x energyRt object (e.g. technology, commodity, supply, …). ... Reserved future use / class-specific arguments. file Optional character string. Path file write script . title: Generate an R script that recreates an energyRt object — design
code: ECOAL <- newTechnology(   name    = "ECOAL",   desc    = "Coal power plant",   input   = data.frame(comm = "COAL", unit = "MMBtu", combustion = 1),   output  = data.frame(comm = "ELC",  unit = "MWh"),   cap2act = 8760,   ceff    = data.frame(comm = "COAL", cinp2use = 1/10),   olife   = data.frame(olife = 30L),   region  = c("R1", "R2") ) design(ECOAL) #> ECOAL <- newTechnology( #>   name = "ECOAL", #>   desc = "Coal power plant", #>   input = data.frame( #>     comm = "COAL", #>     unit = "MMBtu", #>     combustion = 1 #>   ), #>   output = data.frame( #>     comm = "ELC", #>     unit = "MWh" #>   ), #>   ceff = data.frame( #>     comm = "COAL", #>     cinp2use = 0.1 #>   ), #>   olife = data.frame( #>     olife = 30L #>   ), #>   cap2act = 8760, #>   region = c("R1", "R2") #> )

code: STG_ELC <- newStorage(  name = "STG_ELC", # used in sets  desc = "Electricity storage (battery)", # for own reference  commodity = "ELECTRICITY", # must match the commodity name in the model  aux = data.frame(    acomm = "LITHIUM", # auxiliary commodity for battery production    unit = "ton" # unit of the auxiliary commodity  ),  start = data.frame(    start = 2020 # the first year of the process is available for installation  ),  end = data.frame(    end = 2030 # last year of the process is available for installation  ),  olife = data.frame(    olife = 20 # operational life of the storage in years  ),  seff = data.frame(    stgeff = 0.999, # storage efficiency    inpeff = 0.9, # charging efficiency    outeff = 0.9 # discharging efficiency  ),  aeff = data.frame(    acomm = "LITHIUM", # track lithium use for battery production    ncap2ainp = convert(4 * 250, "Wh/kg", "GWh/kt") # lithium per energy capacity  ),  af = data.frame(    # af.lo = 0., # lower bound for the capacity factor    af.up = 1. # upper bound for the capacity factor  ),  fixom = data.frame(    #region = "R1",    #year = 2020,    fixom = 0.9 # fixed operation and maintenance cost  ),  cap2stg = 4, # four-hours of storage  invcost = data.frame(    region = c("R1", NA), # region R1 and all other regions    invcost = c(1e3, 1.1e3) # investment cost in MUSD/GWh of 4-hour storage  ),  fullYear = TRUE, # full year storage cycle  weather = data.frame(    weather = "AMBIENT_TEMP", # weather factor for capacity factor    waf.up = 1 # affects upper boundary of capacity factor    # waf.lo = 0.9 # affects lower boundary of capacity factor  )  # region = c("R1", "R2", "R3"), ) draw(STG_ELC) #> Error: unable to find an inherited method for function ‘draw’ for signature ‘obj = "storage"’

what: Compare interpolation settings (size & build time) for a model — compare_interp_settings
title: Compare interpolation settings (size & build time) for a model — compare_interp_settings
text: Interpolates mod memory several setting combinations tabulates big resulting model : total value-parameter rows, parameter / map / set counts, variable & constraint estimate (model_size()), -memory size, build time. quick way see fold / sparse / prune trade model size speed committing solve.

title: Compare interpolation settings (size & build time) for a model — compare_interp_settings
code: compare_interp_settings(   mod,   settings = NULL,   ...,   name = "cmp",   verbose = FALSE,   top_n = 12L,   keep_scen = FALSE )

title: Compare interpolation settings (size & build time) for a model — compare_interp_settings
text: mod model. settings named list setting combinations; element list arguments forwarded interpolate_model(), e.g. list(fold = TRUE, sparse = TRUE, prune = TRUE). NULL default grid (dense / sparse / sparse+prune / fold+sparse+prune / fold-) used. ... arguments forwarded EVERY interp_mod() call, e.g. horizon = newHorizon(period = 2024). name base scenario name; build "<name>_<setting>". verbose forwarded interpolate_model() (per-build progress). top_n number largest parameters keep per build. keep_scen TRUE, interpolated scenarios returned $scen (handy feed later solve step). default save memory.

title: Compare interpolation settings (size & build time) for a model — compare_interp_settings
text: object class interp_settings_cmp: list summary — one row per setting (ok, seconds, mb, param_rows, counts, variable/constraint estimates, error), top — wide data.frame largest parameters' row counts per setting, details — named list per-build model_size() objects, scen — named list scenarios keep_scen = TRUE, settings — settings grid used.

title: Compare interpolation settings (size & build time) for a model — compare_interp_settings
text: builds use ondisk = FALSE (sizes measured memory directly comparable) overwrite = TRUE.

title: Compare interpolation settings (size & build time) for a model — compare_interp_settings
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cmp <- compare_interp_settings(mod, horizon = newHorizon(period = 2024)) cmp                       # prints the comparison table # custom grid: compare_interp_settings(mod,   settings = list(     none    = list(fold = FALSE, sparse = FALSE, prune = FALSE),     all     = list(fold = c("region","slice","year","comm","tech","stg","trade"),                    sparse = TRUE, prune = TRUE)),   horizon = newHorizon(period = 2024)) } # }

what: Compare interpolation settings AND solvers for a model — compare_solve_settings
title: Compare interpolation settings AND solvers for a model — compare_solve_settings
text: Interpolates mod setting combination (via compare_interp_settings()) solves every build supplied solver option, tabulating objective, solve time status. solution must depend storage settings, objective identical across fold / sparse / prune given solver – print method flags per-solver disagreement.

title: Compare interpolation settings AND solvers for a model — compare_solve_settings
code: compare_solve_settings(   mod,   settings = NULL,   solvers,   ...,   name = "cmp",   verbose = FALSE,   tmp.dir = NULL )

title: Compare interpolation settings AND solvers for a model — compare_solve_settings
text: mod model. settings named list interp_mod() setting combinations (see compare_interp_settings()); NULL uses default grid. solvers list solver-option objects, e.g. list(solver_options$glpk, solver_options$julia_highs). Names taken list names present, otherwise option's $name. ... forwarded every interp_mod() build (e.g. horizon = newHorizon(period = 2024)). name base scenario name. verbose forwarded interp_mod(). tmp.dir solver working directory (passed solve_scen()); NULL lets solver pick one.

title: Compare interpolation settings AND solvers for a model — compare_solve_settings
text: object class solve_settings_cmp: summary (one row per setting x solver: ok, seconds, objective, error), interp (interp-size table), plus top / details interpolation comparison.

title: Draw a schematic representation of a process — draw
code: TECH01 <- newTechnology(   "TECH01",   desc = "Technology Description",   input = data.frame(     comm = c("COM1", "COM2", "COM5", "COM7", "COM8", "COM9"),     group = c("1", "1", NA, "2", "2", "2"),     unit = c("unit1", "unit2", "unit5", "unit7", "unit8", "unit9")   ),   output = data.frame(     comm = c("COM3", "COM4", "COM6"),     group = c("3", NA, "3"),     unit = c("unit3", "unit4", "unit6")   ),   group = data.frame(     group = c("1", "2", "3"),     desc = c("Group1", "Group2", "Group3"),     unit = "unit"   ),   aux = data.frame(     acomm = c("AUX1", "AUX2", "AUX3", "AUX4"),     unit = c("unit1", "unit2", "unit3", "unit4")   ),   region = c("R1", "R2", "R3"),   geff = data.frame(     group = c("1", "2"),     ginp2use = c(0.12, 0.789)   ),   ceff = data.frame(     comm = c("COM1", "COM2", "COM5", "COM7", "COM8", "COM9", "COM3", "COM4", "COM6"),     cinp2ginp = c(.1, .2, NA, .7, .8, .9, rep(NA, 3)),     cinp2use = c(NA, NA, .5, NA, NA, NA, rep(NA, 3)),     use2cact = c(rep(NA, 6), .36, .4, .36),     cact2cout = c(rep(NA, 6), .3, NA, .6),     share.lo = c(.01, .02, NA, .07, .08, .0, .03, NA, .06),     share.up = c(.91, .92, NA, .97, .98, 1, .83, NA, .96)   ),   aeff = data.frame(     acomm = c("AUX1", "AUX2", "AUX3", "AUX4"),     comm = c(NA, "COM1", NA, "COM3"),     act2ainp = c(1, NA, NA, NA),     cinp2aout = c(NA, 2, NA, NA),     cap2aout = c(NA, NA, 3, NA),     cout2aout = c(NA, NA, NA, 4)   ),   weather = data.frame(     weather = "WEATHER_CF1",     waf.up = .99   ) ) draw(TECH01)  STG_ELC <- newStorage(   name = "STG_ELC", # used in sets   desc = "Electricity storage (battery)", # for own reference   commodity = "ELECTRICITY", # must match the commodity name in the model   aux = data.frame(     acomm = "LITHIUM", # auxiliary commodity for battery production     unit = "ton" # unit of the auxiliary commodity   ),   start = data.frame(     start = 2020 # the first year of the process is available for installation   ),   end = data.frame(     end = 2030 # last year of the process is available for installation   ),   olife = data.frame(     olife = 20 # operational life of the storage in years   ),   seff = data.frame(     stgeff = 0.999, # storage efficiency     inpeff = 0.9, # charging efficiency     outeff = 0.9 # discharging efficiency   ),   aeff = data.frame(     acomm = "LITHIUM", # track lithium use for battery production     ncap2ainp = convert(4 * 250, "Wh/kg", "GWh/kt") # lithium per energy capacity   ),   af = data.frame(     # af.lo = 0., # lower bound for the capacity factor     af.up = 1. # upper bound for the capacity factor   ),   fixom = data.frame(     # region = "R1",     # year = 2020,     fixom = 0.9 # fixed operation and maintenance cost   ),   cap2stg = 4, # four-hours of storage   invcost = data.frame(     region = c("R1", NA), # region R1 and all other regions     invcost = c(1e3, 1.1e3) # investment cost in MUSD/GWh of 4-hour storage   ),   fullYear = TRUE, # full year storage cycle   weather = data.frame(     weather = "AMBIENT_TEMP", # weather factor for capacity factor     waf.up = 1 # affects upper boundary of capacity factor     # waf.lo = 0.9 # affects lower boundary of capacity factor   )   # region = c("R1", "R2", "R3"), ) draw(STG_ELC)   SUP_COA <- newSupply(   name = "SUP_COA",   desc = "Coal supply",   commodity = "COA",   unit = "PJ",   reserve = data.frame(     region = c("R1", "R2", "R3"),     res.up = c(2e5, 1e4, 3e6) # total reserves/deposits   ),   availability = data.frame(     region = c("R1", "R2", "R3"),     year = NA_integer_,     slice = "ANNUAL",     ava.up = c(1e3, 1e2, 2e2), # annual availability     cost = c(10, 20, 30) # cost of the resource (currency per unit)   ),   region = c("R1", "R2", "R3") ) draw(SUP_COA)  DSTEEL <- newDemand(   name = "DSTEEL",   desc = "Steel demand",   commodity = "STEEL",   unit = "Mt",   dem = data.frame(     region = "UTOPIA", # NA for every region     year = c(2020, 2030, 2050),     slice = "ANNUAL",     dem = c(100, 200, 300)   ),   region = "UTOPIA", # optional, to narrow the specification of the demand ) draw(DSTEEL)  EXPOIL <- newExport(   name = "EXPOIL", # used in sets   desc = "Oil export from the model to RoW", # for own reference   commodity = "OIL", # must match the commodity name in the model   unit = "Mtoe", # for own reference   exp = data.frame(     region = rep(c("R1", "R2"), each = 2), # export region(s)     year = rep(c(2020, 2050)), # export years     price = 500, # export price in MUSD/Mtoe (USD/t),     exp.up = rep(c(1e3, 1e4), each = 2), # upper bound for export in each year     exp.lo = rep(c(5e2, 0), each = 2) # lower bound for export in each year   ) ) draw(EXPOIL)  IMPOIL <- newImport(   name = "IMPOIL", # used in sets   desc = "Oil import to the model to RoW", # for own reference   commodity = "OIL", # must match the commodity name in the model   unit = "Mtoe", # for own reference   imp = data.frame(     region = rep(c("R1", "R2"), each = 2), # import region(s)     year = rep(c(2020, 2050)), # import years     price = 600, # import price in MUSD/Mtoe (USD/t),     imp.up = rep(c(1e4, 1e6), each = 2), # upper bound for import in each year     imp.lo = rep(c(1e4, 1e5), each = 2) # lower bound for import in each year   ) ) draw(IMPOIL)  PIPELINE2 <- newTrade(   name = "PIPELINE2",   desc = "Some transport pipeline",   commodity = "OIL",   routes = data.frame(     src = c("R1", "R1", "R2", "R3"),     dst = c("R2", "R3", "R3", "R2")   ),   trade = data.frame(     src = c("R1", "R1", "R2", "R3"),     dst = c("R2", "R3", "R3", "R2"),     teff = c(0.912, 0.913, 0.923, 0.932)   ),   aux = data.frame(     acomm = c("ELC", "CH4"),     unit = c("MWh", "kt")   ),   aeff = data.frame(     acomm = c("ELC", "CH4", "ELC", "CH4"),     src = c("R1", "R1", "R2", "R3"),     dst = c("R2", "R2", "R3", "R2"),     csrc2ainp = c(.5, NA, .3, NA),     cdst2ainp = c(.4, NA, .6, NA),     csrc2aout = c(NA, .1, NA, .2)   ),   olife = list(olife = 60) ) #> Warning: NAs introduced by coercion to integer range #> Warning: NAs introduced by coercion to integer range draw(PIPELINE2, node = "R1")  draw(PIPELINE2, node = "R2")  draw(PIPELINE2, node = "R3")

what: Extract data from energyRt objects — getData
title: Extract data from energyRt objects — getData
text: Generic accessor. Dispatches object class: scenario (list scenarios): interpolated/solved data modInp/modOut – see scenario method . model objects (technology, commodity, storage, supply, demand, trade, ...): object's raw input slot data (pre-interpolation). model / repository: raw input slots stacked across contained objects. Model-object methods (technology, commodity, storage, supply, demand, trade) return object's raw input slot data. model/repository stack across contained objects. Use name select slot(s) (e.g. "invcost"), ... filter (region=, year=, comm=, col_=regex), merge=TRUE one long tidy frame (param/value), interpolate=TRUE expand year-bearing slots years (observed year range) parameter's default interpolation rule.

title: Extract data from energyRt objects — getData
text: scen Object scenario list scenarios. ... filters various sets (setname = c(val1, val2) setname_ = "matching pattern"), see details. name character vector names parameters /variables. merge TRUE, search results merged one dataframe; named list returned FALSE. TRUE, data.frame (empty nothing matched) always returned, never NULL. timeframe controls sub-annual time aggregation results carry slice column. One "lowest" (default, aggregate/sum flows coarsest level, normally ANNUAL), "highest" (native/finest, stored), "" (return every timeframe level stacked), explicit calendar level name (e.g. "SEASON", "YDAY") aggregate level. Non-slice data, state/level variables (e.g. vStorageStore) summing slices meaningless, returned unchanged. process TRUE, dimensions "tech", "stg", "trade", "imp", "expp", "dem", "sup" renamed "process". parameters TRUE, parameters included search returned found. variables TRUE, variables included search returned found. maps TRUE, map-type parameters (membership mappings, value column) also returned. ignore.case grepl parameter regular expressions used '...' 'name_'. newNames renaming sets, named character vector list new names values, old names names - input parameter renameSets function. operation performed merging data (merge parameter). newValues revalue sets, named character vector list new values values, old values names - input parameter revalueSets function. operation performed merging data (merge parameter). na.rm TRUE, NA values dropped. digits integer, indicates number decimal places rounding, NULL - actions. drop.zeros logical, rows containing zero values filtered . asTibble logical, data.frames converted tibbles. stringsAsFactors logical, sets values converted factors? yearsAsFactors logical, year converted factors? Set 'year' integer default. scenNameInList logical, name scenarios used provided list several scenarios? verbose logical, print progress diagnostic messages. interpolate TRUE, expand year-bearing object slots target years using parameter's default interpolation rule (quick demonstration plots). Default FALSE (raw data stored). meaningful object/model/repository methods. years integer vector target milestone years interpolate (default: yearly range observed data).

title: Extract data from energyRt objects — getData
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data("utopia_scen_BAU.RData") getData(scen, name = "pDemand", year = 2015, merge = TRUE) getData(scen, name = "vTechOut", comm = "ELC", merge = TRUE, year = 2015) elc2050 <- getData(scen, parameters = FALSE, comm = "ELC", year = 2050) names(elc2050) elc2050$vBalance } # }
