what: energyRt: Energy Systems Modeling Toolbox — energyRt-package
title: energyRt: Energy Systems Modeling Toolbox — energyRt-package
text: macro-language energy system modeling R. Describe energy system R objects (commodities, technologies, supply, demand, storage, trade), compile capacity-expansion dispatch optimization model, solve one four interchangeable backends (GLPK/MathProg, Julia/JuMP, Python/Pyomo, GAMS), analyze tidy results built-tools levelized costs, technology reports, plotting.

title: energyRt: Energy Systems Modeling Toolbox — energyRt-package
text: Maintainer: Oleg Lugovoy olugovoy@optimalsolution.dev (ORCID) Authors: Oleg Lugovoy olugovoy@optimalsolution.dev (ORCID) Vladimir Potashnikov potashnikov.vu@gmail.com contributors: Tarun Sharma tarunsharma@ms.iitr.ac.(ORCID) [contributor]

what: Extract a tidy mix (generation, capacity, fuel) from a solved scenario — getMix
title: Extract a tidy mix (generation, capacity, fuel) from a solved scenario — getMix
text: Collects building blocks "mix" chart solved scenario one tidy data.frame: technology output, storage charge/discharge, inter-regional rest--world trade, demand. extractor feeds autoplot(scenario, ...), scenario report, custom analysis (including sonification – see useR! training course).

title: Extract a tidy mix (generation, capacity, fuel) from a solved scenario — getMix
code: getMix(   scen,   type = c("generation", "capacity", "new_capacity", "fuel"),   comm = "ELC",   region = NULL,   year = NULL,   slice = NULL,   drop_small = 0 )

title: Extract a tidy mix (generation, capacity, fuel) from a solved scenario — getMix
text: scen solved scenario object, named list scenarios (results row-bound list names scenario column). type character: "generation" (default – output comm process, storage-exports negative), "capacity" (vTechCap + vStorageCap), "new_capacity" (vTechNewCap + vStorageNewCap), "fuel" (technology fuel consumption vTechInp input commodity). comm character, balanced commodity "generation" (default "ELC"). Ignored types. region character vector NULL (regions). Inter-regional trade flows meaningful region subset skipped region = NULL (cancel -region sum). year integer vector NULL (milestone years). slice NULL annual sums, regular expression selecting slice sample (e.g. "^SUM_" summer day utopia_s4h24 calendar). matched slices carry hour tag ("_h00"..."_h23"), integer hour column added. drop_small numeric [0, 1): drop processes whose total absolute value share largest process (default 0, keep ).

title: Extract a tidy mix (generation, capacity, fuel) from a solved scenario — getMix
text: tidy data.frame columns scenario, type, process, flow (generation, storage-/, import/export, demand, fuel, capacity, new_capacity), comm, region, year, value, – slice given – slice (+ hour parsable). Missing variables skipped silently (e.g. model without storage trade).

title: Extract a tidy mix (generation, capacity, fuel) from a solved scenario — getMix
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ gen <- getMix(scen, "generation")                      # annual, all regions day <- getMix(scen, "generation", slice = "^SUM_")     # summer-day dispatch cmp <- getMix(list(BASE = s1, CO2CAP = s2), "capacity") } # }

what: Retrieve model objects from a repository, model or scenario — getObject
title: Retrieve model objects from a repository, model or scenario — getObject
text: getObject() returns model building-block objects held container – commodities, technologies, supplies, storages, – selected class, name, description, region /object slot. object-level counterpart getData(), returns objects' parameter data.

title: Retrieve model objects from a repository, model or scenario — getObject
code: getObject(x, ...)  # Default S3 method getObject(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'repository' getObject(   x,   class = NULL,   name = NULL,   desc = NULL,   region = NULL,   ...,   regex = FALSE,   ignore.case = TRUE,   region_agnostic = TRUE,   drop = FALSE )  # S3 method for class 'model' getObject(   x,   class = NULL,   name = NULL,   desc = NULL,   region = NULL,   ...,   regex = FALSE,   ignore.case = TRUE,   region_agnostic = TRUE,   drop = FALSE )  # S3 method for class 'scenario' getObject(   x,   class = NULL,   name = NULL,   desc = NULL,   region = NULL,   ...,   regex = FALSE,   ignore.case = TRUE,   region_agnostic = TRUE,   drop = FALSE )

title: Retrieve model objects from a repository, model or scenario — getObject
text: x repository, model scenario. ... additional per-slot filters forwarded matching engine, e.g. timeframe = "HOUR". Character filters exact default; data.frame slot filtered named columns. Filtering slot implicitly restricts results classes . class character vector object classes keep (e.g. "technology", c("supply", "import")); NULL (default) keeps classes. name character, object name(s) match @name. desc character, description pattern(s) match @desc. region character, keep objects belonging regions. Region-agnostic objects (e.g. commodities, carry region) kept every region unless region_agnostic = FALSE. regex logical; treat name, desc character ... filters regular expressions instead exact matches (default FALSE). ignore.case logical; case-insensitive matching regex = TRUE (default TRUE). region_agnostic logical; whether objects region information satisfy region filter (default TRUE). drop logical; TRUE exactly one object matches, return object instead one-element list (default FALSE).

title: Retrieve model objects from a repository, model or scenario — getObject
text: named list model objects keyed @name (empty list none match); single object drop = TRUE exactly one matches.

title: Retrieve model objects from a repository, model or scenario — getObject
text: Class, name, description slot matched engine powers internal object accessors; region filter applied get_region(), reads regions uniformly @region slots region/src/dst columns data.frame slots, works every class (including import/export/trade, store region structurally). scenario unwrapped model automatically.

title: Retrieve model objects from a repository, model or scenario — getObject
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ repo <- utopia_modules$electricity$reg3$repo getObject(repo, class = "technology")                 # all technologies getObject(repo, class = c("supply", "commodity"))     # two classes getObject(repo, region = "R1")                         # everything in R1 getObject(repo, name = "ECOA", drop = TRUE)            # the ECOA object getObject(repo, desc = "coal", regex = TRUE)           # by description getObject(repo, class = "technology", timeframe = "HOUR")  # slot filter } # }

title: Retrieve slot details in rd-format — get_slot_doc
code: slotNames("technology") #>  [1] "name"               "desc"               "input"              #>  [4] "output"             "aux"                "units"              #>  [7] "group"              "cap2act"            "geff"               #> [10] "ceff"               "aeff"               "af"                 #> [13] "afs"                "weather"            "fixom"              #> [16] "varom"              "invcost"            "start"              #> [19] "end"                "olife"              "capacity"           #> [22] "optimizeRetirement" "fullYear"           "timeframe"          #> [25] "region"             "misc"               get_slot_doc("technology", "input") |> cat() #> data.frame. Main commodities input. Main commodities are linked to the process capacity and activity. Their parameters are defined in the `ceff` slot. #>   \describe{ #>     \item{comm}{character. Name of the input commodity.} #>     \item{unit}{character. Unit of the input commodity.} #>     \item{group}{character. Name of input-commodities-group.} #>     \item{combustion}{numeric. combustion factor from 0 to 1 (default 1) to calculate emissions from fuels combustion (commodities intermediate consumption, more broadly) #> } #>  } get_slot_doc("technology", "capacity") |> cat() #> data.frame. Capacity of the installed technology (in units of capacity). #>   \describe{ #>     \item{region}{character. Region name to apply the parameter, NA for every region.} #>     \item{year}{integer. Year to apply the parameter, required, values between specified years will be interpolated.} #>     \item{stock}{numeric. Predefined capacity of the technology in units of capacity, default is 0. This parameter also defines the exogenous capacity retirement (age-based), or exogenous capacity additions, not optimized by the model, and not included in investment costs. #> } #>     \item{cap.lo}{numeric. Lower bound on the total capacity (preexisting stock and new installations), ignored if NA.} #>     \item{cap.up}{numeric. Upper bound on the total capacity (preexisting stock and new installations), ignored if NA.} #>     \item{cap.fx}{numeric. Fixed total capacity (preexisting stock and new installations), ignored if NA. This parameter overrides `cap.lo` and `cap.up`.} #>     \item{ncap.lo}{numeric. Lower bound on the new capacity (new installations), ignored if NA.} #>     \item{ncap.up}{numeric. Upper bound on the new capacity (new installations), ignored if NA.} #>     \item{ncap.fx}{numeric. Fixed new capacity (new installations), ignored if NA. This parameter overrides `ncap.lo` and `ncap.up`.} #>     \item{ret.lo}{numeric. Lower bound on the capacity retirement (age-based), ignored if NA.} #>     \item{ret.up}{numeric. Upper bound on the capacity retirement (age-based), ignored if NA.} #>     \item{ret.fx}{numeric. Fixed capacity retirement (age-based), ignored if NA. This parameter overrides `ret.lo` and `ret.up`.} #>  } get_slot_doc("demand", "dem") |> cat() #> data.frame. Specification of the demand. #>   \describe{ #>     \item{region}{character. Name of region for the demand value. NA for every region.} #>     \item{year}{integer. Year of the demand. NA for every year.} #>     \item{slice}{character. Name of the slice for the demand value. NA for every slice.} #>     \item{dem}{numeric. Value of the demand.} #>  } get_slot_doc("commodity", "agg") |> cat() #> data.frame. Used to define an aggregation of several commodities into the `name` commodity. #>  #>   \describe{ #>     \item{comm}{character. Name of a commodity being aggregated.} #>     \item{unit}{character. Unit of the commodity being aggregated.} #>     \item{agg}{numeric. weight of the commodity in the aggregation, must be set for all aggregated commodities. #> } #>  }

what: Interpolate a model into a solver-ready scenario — interpolate_model
title: Interpolate a model into a solver-ready scenario — interpolate_model
text: Builds interpolated scenario model via mapping pipeline: collects sets model objects, builds membership / calendar / lifespan / value / constraint / cost mappings, extracts interpolates numeric parameters milestone years, (optionally) folds, prunes validates result. returned scenario ready solve_model() / solve_scenario().

title: Interpolate a model into a solver-ready scenario — interpolate_model
code: interpolate_model(   mod,   name = NULL,   ...,   desc = NULL,   ondisk = FALSE,   overwrite = FALSE,   fold = FALSE,   sparse = TRUE,   prune = TRUE,   validate = TRUE,   code = NULL,   verbose = getOption("energyRt.verbose", FALSE) )

title: Interpolate a model into a solver-ready scenario — interpolate_model
text: mod model object, scenario (@model re-interpolated). name character scenario name. NULL, default scen_<model> used (warning). ... additional energyRt objects folded model pipeline runs: settings, config, calendar, horizon, whole repository, individual model "bricks" (technology, commodity, storage, ...). scenario overrides extends model (e.g. pass sampled calendar interpolate reduced time resolution). desc character scenario description. ondisk logical; store parameter's data -disk parameter store rather -memory @data slot. FALSE (default) keeps data memory, solver writers read directly; TRUE suits large models (data materialised back memory solve time). overwrite logical; overwrite existing -disk scenario name. fold logical character; whole-column "fold" trimmable dimensions NA wildcards shrink data. TRUE folds region + slice; FALSE (default) folds nothing; character vector selects dims among region, slice, year, comm, tech, stg, trade. folded scenario expanded solver-ready form solve time. sparse logical; storage knob. TRUE drops value == defVal rows (folds); FALSE materialises default parameter's full domain (unfolds). prune logical; drop interpolated rows fall outside equation-domain maps (effect solution, smaller data). validate logical; run post-interpolation consistency checks (schema, duplicate keys, map/parameter coverage). code optional named list overriding solver source-code blocks (GLPK, GAMS, JuMP, PYOMOConcrete, ...), either script-file path character vector lines. Lets model-script version supplied interpolation time without rebuilding sysdata (handy /B templates). verbose logical; print per-step progress.

title: Interpolate a model into a solver-ready scenario — interpolate_model
text: interpolated scenario object.

what: Levelized cost of commodity production — levcost
title: Levelized cost of commodity production — levcost
text: Computes levelized cost energy (LCOE) technology, repository, model, scenario object.

title: Levelized cost of commodity production — levcost
code: levcost(object, comm, name, ...)  # S4 method for class 'repository' levcost(object, comm, name, ...)  # S4 method for class 'model' levcost(object, comm, name, ...)  # S4 method for class 'scenario' levcost(object, comm, name, ...)

title: Levelized cost of commodity production — levcost
text: object technology (list thereof), repository, model, solved scenario object. comm Character vector NULL.  Output commodity(ies) use LCOE normalisation.  NULL uses commodities resolved output group. name Character. technology, name tag; repository/model/scenario, name technology / process price. ... Additional arguments passed underlying implementation. technology objects useful : group Character NULL.  Output group name. repo repository list energyRt objects (supplies, commodities, weather) supplement mini-model. fuel_costs Named numeric vector [commodity -> cost] input commodities found repo. autocomplete Logical, default FALSE (repository/model methods). TRUE, input commodities without supply container auto-supplied (zero-cost, priced via fuel_costs) instead returning NULL. discount Numeric (0–1), default 0.05. base_year Integer NULL. horizon horizon object, numeric year vector, NULL (derives @olife). calendar calendar object NULL. timeframe "ANNUAL" (default) "native". "ANNUAL" prices technology single annual time-slice: weather profile collapsed annual capacity factor (applied technology's annual availability), capacity sized serve unit annual demand factor (textbook LCOE). "native" keeps supplied (sub-annual) calendar normalises total generation – useful technology analysed together storage transmission, sub-annual dispatch matters. backstop Logical, default TRUE. Enables expensive dummy-import slack output commodity balance mini-model always solves even technology serve slice ; slack cost excluded LCOE. region Character NULL. weather weather object, list weather objects, NULL. frontier Logical, default FALSE.  TRUE additional solves performed map production frontier technologies multi-commodity grouped output share constraints. solver Solver spec list, default solver_options$glpk. as_scenario Logical, default FALSE.  TRUE full solved scenario returned LCOE tables attached scenario@misc. verbose Logical, default TRUE.

title: Levelized cost of commodity production — levcost
text: technology input: list class "levcost" fields: $levcost data.frame – total levelized cost year. $levcost_npv Named numeric – NPV-weighted average LCOE. $cost_breakdown data.frame – tidy cost components year. Components: eac, fixom, varom, supply, import, export (negative, credit). $cost_breakdown_npv data.frame – NPV-weighted component breakdown. $cost_yearly data.frame – wide undiscounted cost table activity, capacity, per-commodity outputs inputs. $levcost_per_act data.frame NULL. $frontier data.frame NULL (requires frontier = TRUE multi-commodity grouped output). $scenario solved scenario object. list technology objects: named list class "levcost_list".

title: Levelized cost of commodity production — levcost
text: technology minimal single-technology energyRt model built around technology, solved, LCOE derived resulting cost production variables. repository / model give technology name; method selects related commodity supply objects container prices named technology . input commodity supply container, returns NULL message unless autocomplete = TRUE (adds zero-cost / fuel_costs supply). model method also takes calendar, region, horizon discount rate model's configuration (overridable). scenario ex-post cost named process solved scenario: discounted sum costs (annualised investment vTechEac, vTechFixom, vTechVarom, plus attributed fuel cost) divided discounted output. Fuel attributed vTechInp; technology grouped input (whose per-commodity consumption solution variable) therefore reports fuel component.

title: Levelized cost of commodity production — levcost
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ lc <- levcost(my_tech, discount = 0.07, base_year = 2025) lc$levcost_npv lc$cost_breakdown autoplot(lc) autoplot(lc, type = "npv")  # List of technologies (each solved independently): lc_list <- levcost(list(tech1, tech2), discount = 0.07) autoplot(lc_list, type = "npv") } # }

title: NEOS Server client (query the remote solver service) — neos

title: NEOS Server client (query the remote solver service) — neos
text: neos_ping(): status string (invisibly), TRUE server alive. neos_list_categories(): named character vector (abbrev -> full name). neos_list_solvers(): character vector solver:inputMethod strings. neos_get_template(): XML job template single string.

title: NEOS Server client (query the remote solver service) — neos

what: Submit and manage a NEOS job — neos_job
title: Submit and manage a NEOS job — neos_job
text: Low-level wrappers NEOS job API. neos_submit_job() submits job document returns (job, password) handle; others use handle poll status retrieve results. Submitting job sends model public service — see notes neos.

title: Submit and manage a NEOS job — neos_job
code: neos_submit_job(xml, user = NULL, password = NULL, timeout = 600)  neos_job_status(job, pw, timeout = 30)  neos_completion_code(job, pw, timeout = 30)  neos_final_results(job, pw, timeout = 120)  neos_get_output_file(job, pw, fileName, timeout = 120)  neos_wait(job, pw, poll = 5, max_wait = 600, verbose = TRUE)

title: Submit and manage a NEOS job — neos_job
text: xml character. job document (see neos_build_gams_xml()). user, password NEOS account credentials authenticatedSubmitJob; NULL, anonymous submitJob used. timeout per-request timeout seconds. job integer job number, pw password, neos_submit_job(). fileName name output file fetch (e.g. "soln.gdx"). poll, max_wait polling interval / overall wait cap seconds. verbose print status waiting.

title: Submit and manage a NEOS job — neos_job
text: neos_submit_job(): list(job, password). neos_job_status() / neos_completion_code(): status string. neos_final_results(): combined solver output text. neos_get_output_file(): raw bytes (e.g. GDX) — write writeBin(). neos_wait(): final status (invisibly) "Done".

title: Create constraint object to add custom constraints to the model. — newConstraint
text: name character. Name constraint object, used sets. desc character. Description constraint. ... named unnamed list(s) left-hand side (LHS) linear terms (summands) define constraint. Every summand defined list following elements: variable - name variable summand. mult - multiplier variable summand. .sum - list sets summand defined. summands can passed named unnamed lists. added lhs slot constraint object linear terms mulipliers variables. eq Type relation ('==' default, '<=', '>=') .list data.frame sets define dimension constraint. rhs numeric value, list data frame sets numeric values constraint. Note: zero values replaced replace_zerros avoid dropping interpolation algorithms. defVal numeric. default value rhs. recommended set default value rhs every constraint avoid unexpected behavior. specified, default value 0, warning issued. interpolation character. Interpolation rule constraint. Recognized values, combination "back", "inter", "forth", e.g., "back.inter" "forth.inter", indicating direction interpolation. default value "inter", meaning interpolation done years specified values. "back" "forth" values induce backward forward interpolation rhs values, respectively. replace_zerros numeric value replace zero values rhs defVal. Default 1e-20. object R object

code: PIPELINE1 <- newTrade(   name = "PIPELINE1",   desc = "Some transport pipeline",   commodity = "OIL",   routes = data.frame(     src = c("R1", "R2"),     dst = c("R2", "R3")   ),   trade = data.frame(     src = c("R1", "R2"),     dst = c("R2", "R3"),     teff = c(0.99, 0.98)   ),   olife = list(olife = 60) ) #> Warning: NAs introduced by coercion to integer range #> Warning: NAs introduced by coercion to integer range draw(PIPELINE1)   PIPELINE2 <- newTrade(   name = "PIPELINE2",   desc = "Some transport pipeline",   commodity = "OIL",   routes = data.frame(     src = c("R1", "R1", "R2", "R3"),     dst = c("R2", "R3", "R3", "R2")   ),   trade = data.frame(     src = c("R1", "R1", "R2", "R3"),     dst = c("R2", "R3", "R3", "R2"),     teff = c(0.912, 0.913, 0.923, 0.932)   ),   aux = data.frame(     acomm = c("ELC", "CH4"),     unit = c("MWh", "kt")   ),   aeff = data.frame(     acomm = c("ELC", "CH4", "ELC", "CH4"),     src = c("R1", "R1", "R2", "R3"),     dst = c("R2", "R2", "R3", "R2"),     csrc2ainp = c(.5, NA, .3, NA),     cdst2ainp = c(.4, NA, .6, NA),     csrc2aout = c(NA, .1, NA, .2)   ),   olife = list(olife = 60) ) #> Warning: NAs introduced by coercion to integer range #> Warning: NAs introduced by coercion to integer range draw(PIPELINE2, node = "R1")  draw(PIPELINE2, node = "R2")  draw(PIPELINE2, node = "R3")

title: Visualize a Calendar object — plot,calendar-method
text: Draws calendar's time-structure stacked rows (one per timeframe, ANNUAL top), rectangle time-slice sized share year. autoplot() ggplot2-idiomatic entry point returns ggplot object plot().

title: Visualize a Calendar object — plot,calendar-method
code: # S4 method for class 'calendar' plot(   x,   ...,   fill = c("order", "share", "weight"),   color_pattern = c("within", "global"),   palette = "D",   labels = TRUE,   label_by = c("name", "slice", "none"),   label_color = "auto",   max_labels = 60L,   border = NA,   show_leafs = NULL,   reference = NULL )  # S3 method for class 'calendar' autoplot(   object,   ...,   fill = c("order", "share", "weight"),   color_pattern = c("within", "global"),   palette = "D",   labels = TRUE,   label_by = c("name", "slice", "none"),   label_color = "auto",   max_labels = 60L,   border = NA,   show_leafs = NULL,   reference = NULL )

title: Visualize a Calendar object — plot,calendar-method
text: x, object object class calendar. ... Passed plot_calendar(). fill One "order" (chronology, default), "share" (year-share slice), "weight" — metric mapped rectangle fill color. color_pattern fill = "order", color gradient applied: "within" (default) colors level slices — full h00→h23 gradient recycled every day, d001→d365 year — row shows cyclical structure; "global" colors absolute chronology (leaf order 1…n, e.g. 0…8760). Ignored "share"/"weight". palette Viridis color option (e.g. "D", "C", "magma") fill scale. labels Logical; draw labels inside rectangles (master /). label_by label rectangle : "name" (default) individual level name (e.g. HOUR cells become h00…h23, YDAY cells d001…d365), "slice" full slice path (e.g. d001_h00), "none". label_color Text color labels. "auto" (default) contrasts label cell — white darker part gradient, dark lighter part — labels stay readable dark fills. Pass single color (e.g. "black", "white") use labels. max_labels Integer; timeframes slices left unlabeled avoid clutter. border Rectangle outline color. NA (default) draws outline, high-resolution row (e.g. 8760 hourly slices) reads smooth gradient instead solid block borders. Pass e.g. "grey30" outline slices coarse calendars. show_leafs Select slices draw (NULL, default, shows ). Two forms: unnamed vector filtering finest (leaf) level — leaf slice names (e.g. "d001_h05") integer leaf indices (e.g. 1:100 first 100 leaves); named list filtering per timeframe level, combined , e.g. list(YDAY = "d100", HOUR = 5:10) — level character vector level's slice names integer positions among slices (HOUR = 5:10 selects 5th–10th hours). kept slices packed left--right x-axis spans total year-share. Colors stay stable (e.g. h05 keeps color whether hours shown). reference Optional full calendar. supplied, x treated subset reference: plot lays reference's full structure fills slices present x (matched slice name), leaving unselected slices empty. Use see part full calendar sampled/subset calendar covers.

title: Visualize a Calendar object — plot,calendar-method
text: ggplot object.

title: Visualize a Calendar object — plot,calendar-method
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cal <- newCalendar(make_timetable(timeslices3), name = "m12h24") plot(cal) autoplot(cal, fill = "share")  # Subset view: show which slices a reduced calendar covers within the full one autoplot(calendars$d365_h24_subset_1day_per_month,          reference = calendars$d365_h24)  # Zoom into specific slices: day 100, hours 5-10 autoplot(calendars$d365_h24, show_leafs = list(YDAY = "d100", HOUR = 5:10)) } # }

title: Heatmap of timeslice-indexed values over a calendar — plot_heatmap
text: Lays timeslice-indexed values 2-D grid whose axes follow calendar structure: finest timeframe becomes y-axis, next-finest x-axis, coarser level(s) become facets — e.g. d365_h24 calendar, x = day--year, y = hour--day. Useful load curves, renewable profiles, prices, sub-annual series.

title: Heatmap of timeslice-indexed values over a calendar — plot_heatmap
text: x data.frame slice column numeric value column, named numeric vector (names slices), calendar object (slice share — value column — shown). calendar calendar object giving layout (matched x slice), format string (e.g. "d365_h24"). NULL, format guessed slice names tsl_guess_format(). value Name value column x (defaults single numeric column, share calendar). facet Optional timeframe level(s) facet . "month" day--year format splits year monthly panels (x = day--month). palette Viridis color option fill scale. name Legend title (defaults value name).

what: Generate a summary report for an energyRt object — report
title: Generate a summary report for an energyRt object — report
text: Creates PDF, HTML, LaTeX document summarising key parameters object.  layout content controlled template argument. technology, datasheet describes technology. repository, model, solved scenario, give technology via name = : datasheet produced, embedded levelized cost computed container / solution (scenario method uses ex-post cost solved model). Without name, container methods report whole object: model / repository full model report – configuration (regions, horizon, calendar, discount), inventory commodities / supplies / demands / trade / constraints, process availability windows chart, every technology storage described one--one (diagram + key parameters). scenario results overview solved scenario – solve status objective, generation / capacity / new-capacity mixes (via getMix autoplot), sub-annual dispatch profile calendar one, emissions cost tables.

title: Generate a summary report for an energyRt object — report
code: report(   object,   template = NULL,   image_file = NULL,   file = NULL,   format = c("html", "pdf", "tex"),   levcost = NULL,   cost_unit = NULL,   open = interactive(),   ... )  # S4 method for class 'technology' report(   object,   template = NULL,   image_file = NULL,   file = NULL,   format = c("html", "pdf", "tex"),   levcost = NULL,   cost_unit = NULL,   open = interactive(),   ... )  # S4 method for class 'repository' report(   object,   template = NULL,   image_file = NULL,   file = NULL,   format = c("html", "pdf", "tex"),   levcost = NULL,   cost_unit = NULL,   open = interactive(),   ... )  # S4 method for class 'model' report(   object,   template = NULL,   image_file = NULL,   file = NULL,   format = c("html", "pdf", "tex"),   levcost = NULL,   cost_unit = NULL,   open = interactive(),   ... )  # S4 method for class 'scenario' report(   object,   template = NULL,   image_file = NULL,   file = NULL,   format = c("html", "pdf", "tex"),   levcost = NULL,   cost_unit = NULL,   open = interactive(),   ... )

title: Generate a summary report for an energyRt object — report
text: object energyRt S4 object: technology, repository, model, solved scenario. template Character.  Template name defines parameters display.  Currently "generic" built package.  Pass absolute path custom .Rmd file use template. Default NULL selects "generic" automatically. image_file Character.  Optional path PNG/JPG image displayed upper-right corner page.  NULL skips image. file Character.  Destination file path.  Defaults report_<name> current working directory, extension appropriate format. format Character.  Output format: "html" (default), "pdf", "tex" (standalone LaTeX source).  Multiple values accepted; one file produced per format.  "pdf"/"tex" require LaTeX installation (e.g. tinytex::install_tinytex()) skipped warning none found. levcost levcost (levcost_list) object returned levcost, NULL (default).  NULL levcost keyword arguments passed via ... (e.g. group, repo, discount), levcost() called automatically object arguments. cost_unit Character NULL.  Cost unit label used LCOE axis (e.g. "USD/GJ").  NULL derives label object@units. open Logical.  Open rendered report system browser/viewer done.  Defaults interactive() (opens interactive session, stays quiet scripts knits). ... Arguments forwarded levcost (levcost = NULL levcost parameters provided) /rmarkdown::render().  Known levcost parameter names intercepted automatically; everything else passed renderer. name Character (container/scenario methods). Name technology / process report.

title: Create a new
text: function initializes returns S4 object class technology, representing specific technology given attributes. function arguments slot-names technology class. Every argument specific format described class documentation.

title: Create a new
code: newTechnology(   name = "",   desc = "",   input = data.frame(),   output = data.frame(),   group = data.frame(),   aux = data.frame(),   units = data.frame(),   cap2act = as.numeric(1),   geff = data.frame(),   ceff = data.frame(),   aeff = data.frame(),   af = data.frame(),   afs = data.frame(),   weather = data.frame(),   capacity = data.frame(),   invcost = data.frame(),   fixom = data.frame(),   varom = data.frame(),   olife = data.frame(),   region = character(),   start = data.frame(),   end = data.frame(),   timeframe = character(),   fullYear = TRUE,   optimizeRetirement = FALSE,   misc = list(),   ... )  # S4 method for class 'technology' update(object, ...)

title: Create a new
text: name character. Name technology, used sets. desc character. Optional description technology reference. input data.frame. Main commodities input. Main commodities linked process capacity activity. parameters defined ceff slot. comm character. Name input commodity. unit character. Unit input commodity. group character. Name input-commodities-group. combustion numeric. combustion factor 0 1 (default 1) calculate emissions fuels combustion (commodities intermediate consumption, broadly) output data.frame. Main commodities output. Main commodities linked process capacity activity. parameters defined ceff slot. comm character. Name output commodity. unit character. Unit output commodity. group character. Name output-commodities-group. group data.frame. Details commodity groups defined input output slots (reference). group character. Name group. Must match group names input output slots. desc character. Description group. unit character. Unit group. aux data.frame. Auxilary commodities, input output, parameters defined aeff slot. acomm character. Name auxilary commodity. unit character. Unit auxilary commodity. units data.frame. Key units process activity capacity (reference). capacity character. Unit capacity use character. Unit 'use' (grouped input) applicable. activity character. Unit activity variable technology. costs character. Currency costs variable technology. cap2act numeric. Capacity activity ratio. Default 1. Specifies much product (activity, output commodity identical) produced per unit capacity. geff data.frame. Input-commodity-group efficiency parameters. region character. Name region apply parameter, NA every region. year integer. Year apply parameter, NA every year. slice character. Name slice apply parameter, NA every slice. group character. Name group apply parameter. Required, must match group names input output slots. ginp2use numeric. Group-input--use coefficient, default 1. ceff data.frame. Main commodity activity efficiency parameters. region character. Region name apply parameter, NA every region. year integer. Year apply parameter, NA every year. slice character. Name slice apply parameter, NA every slice. comm character. Name commodity apply parameter, different parameters require specification either input output commodity. cinp2use numeric. Commodity-input--use coefficient, default 1. use2cact numeric. Use--commodity-activity coefficient, default 1. cact2cout numeric. Commodity-activity--commodity-output coefficient, default 1. cinp2ginp numeric. Commodity-input--group-input coefficient, default 1. share.lo numeric. Fixed share commodity within group, ignored NA. parameter overrides share.lo share.. afc.lo numeric. Lower bound physical value commodity, ignored NA. afc.numeric. Technology-new-capacity--auxilary-commodity-input-coefficient, ignored NA. ncap2aout numeric. Technology-new-capacity--auxilary-commodity-output coefficient, ignored NA. af data.frame. title: Create a new
text: object class technology.

title: Create a new
code: ECOAL <- newTechnology(   name = "ECOAL", # name, used in sets, no white spaces or special characters   desc = "Generic coal power plant", # any description of the technology   input = data.frame(     comm = "COAL", # name of input commodity     unit = "MMBtu", # unit of the input commodity     # combustion factor from 0 to 1 (default 1) to calculate emissions     # from fuels combustion (commodities intermediate consumption, more broadly)     combustion = 1   ),   output = data.frame(     comm = "ELC", # name of output commodity     unit = "MWh" # unit of the output commodity   ),   aux = data.frame(     acomm = c("NOx", "SO2", "Hg"), # names of auxilary commodities     unit = c("kg", "kg", "g") # units   ),   # Capacity to activity ration: 8760 MWh output a year per MW of capacity   cap2act = 8760,   ceff = data.frame( # efficiency parameters for the main commodities     comm = "COAL",     # efficiency, 1/10 MWh per MMBtu, inverse heat rate     # check: 1 / convert(10, "MMBtu", "MWh") ~= 34% efficiency     cinp2use = 1 / 10   ),   aeff = data.frame( # paramaters for the auxilary commodities     acomm = c("NOx", "SO2", "Hg"),     act2aout = c(0.1, 0.2, 0.3) # emission factors, linked to activity   ),   af = data.frame( # availability (capacity) factor by time slices     af.up = 0.95 # maximum 95% per hour   ),   afs = data.frame( # availability factor by timeframes     slice = "ANNUAL", # annual availability factor     afs.lo = 0.40, # at least 40% per year     afs.up = 0.85 # maximum 85% per year   ),   fixom = data.frame( # fixed operational and maintenance cost     region = c("R1", "R2", NA), # regions, NA - all other regions     fixom = c(100, 200, 150) # MW a year   ),   varom = data.frame( # variable operational and maintenance cost     region = c("R1", "R2"), # regions     varom = c(1, 2) # $1 and $2 per MWh   ),   invcost = data.frame( # investment cost     year = c(2020, 2030, 2040), # to differentiate by years     invcost = c(1000, 900, 800) # $1000, $900, $800 per MW   ),   start = data.frame( # start year     start = 2020 # can be installed from 2020   ),   end = data.frame( # end year     end = 2040 # can be installed until 2040   ),   olife = data.frame( # operational life     olife = 30 # years   ),   capacity = data.frame( # existing capacity     year = c(2020, 2030, 2040), # to differentiate by years     region = c("R1"), # exists only in R1     stock = c(300, 200, 100) # age-based exogenous retirement   ),   # regions where the technology can be installed   region = c("R1", "R2", "R5", "R7"), ) draw(ECOAL)

title: Common formats of time-slices. — tsl_formats
code: dtm2tsl(lubridate::now()) #> [1] "d186_h16" dtm2tsl(lubridate::ymd("2020-12-31")) #> [1] NA dtm2tsl(lubridate::ymd("2020-12-31"), d366.as.na = FALSE) #> [1] "d366_h00" dtm2tsl(lubridate::now(tzone = "UTC"), format = "d365") #> [1] "d186" dtm2tsl(lubridate::ymd("2020-12-31"), format = "d365") #> [1] NA dtm2tsl(lubridate::ymd("2020-12-31"), format = "d365", d366.as.na = FALSE) #> [1] "d366" dtm2tsl(lubridate::ymd("2020-12-31"), format = "d366") #> [1] "d366" tsl <- c("y2007_d365_h15", NA, "d151_h22", "d001", "m10_h12") tsl2dtm(tsl[1]) #> [1] "2007-12-31 15:00:00 UTC" tsl2dtm(tsl[1:2]) #> Warning:  1 failed to parse. #> [1] "2007-12-31 15:00:00 UTC" NA                        tsl2dtm(tsl[2]) #> NULL tsl2dtm(tsl[3]) #> NULL tsl2dtm(tsl[4]) #> NULL tsl2dtm(tsl[3], year = 2010) #> [1] "2010-05-31 22:00:00 UTC" tsl2dtm(tsl[4], year = 1900) #> [1] "1900-01-01 UTC" tsl2dtm(tsl[3:4], year = 1900) #> NULL

what: Build the UTOPIA model building blocks — utopia_build
title: Build the UTOPIA model building blocks — utopia_build
text: Assembles complete UTOPIA electricity model named list reusable energyRt building blocks – commodity repository, supply repository, demand, weather objects, technologies, storage, ready--solve base repository ($repo), pre-built scenario levers (CO2 cap, carbon tax, renewable-share target, -new-nuclear). Region-dependent inputs (weather, demand, base-year stock) come utopia_profiles().

title: Build the UTOPIA model building blocks — utopia_build
code: utopia_build(   regions = c("reg1", "reg2", "reg3"),   calendar = "utopia_s4h24",   annual_demand = 100,   years = c(2020, 2030, 2040, 2050),   demand_growth = c(1, 1.2, 1.4, 1.6) )

title: Build the UTOPIA model building blocks — utopia_build
text: regions character vector region names. calendar UTOPIA calendar name: "utopia_s4h24" (default), "utopia_m12h24" "utopia_seasons". annual_demand base-year electricity demand per region, PJ (default 100). years milestone years demand/levers (default 2020, 2030, 2040, 2050). demand_growth demand multipliers per milestone (default 1/1.2/1.4/1.6).

title: Build the UTOPIA model building blocks — utopia_build
text: named list ("module kit"): regions, calendar, repo (base repository), repo_comm, repo_supply, DEM_ELC, WSOL/WWIN/ WHYD, technologies (ECOA ... ECOABIO), STG_BTR, levers CO2_CAP, CT_CO2, RES_SHARE, NO_NEW_NUC.

title: UTOPIA input profiles (deterministic) — utopia_profiles
text: Expand saved, region-agnostic UTOPIA profiles (utopia_weather, utopia_demand, utopia_stock) set regions chosen calendar. Replaces vignette's former random generators. weather capacity factors can re-sourced run time IDEEA (source = "ideea"); "saved" (default) uses packaged data never needs external dataset.

title: UTOPIA input profiles (deterministic) — utopia_profiles
code: utopia_profiles(   regions,   calendar = c("utopia_s4h24", "utopia_m12h24", "utopia_seasons"),   source = c("saved", "ideea"),   resources = c(WSOL = "WSOL", WWIN = "WWIN", WHYD = "WHYD"),   cluster = 1L,   diversify = TRUE )

title: UTOPIA input profiles (deterministic) — utopia_profiles
text: regions character vector region names. calendar target resolution: "utopia_s4h24" (4 seasons x 24 hours, 96 slices, default base case), "utopia_m12h24" (12 months x 24 hours, 288) "utopia_seasons" (4 seasons x 3 dayparts, 12). source "saved" (packaged data, default) "ideea" (re-aggregate IDEEA::ideea_modules installed). resources named character vector mapping resource keys (WSOL, WWIN, WHYD) IDEEA element names, used source = "ideea". cluster integer, IDEEA resource cluster use (source = "ideea"). diversify logical (default TRUE): scale solar wind capacity factors deterministic per-region factors (defined UTOPIA map regions R1–R11; names get factor 1), regions different renewable endowments – sunnier south, windier coast. FALSE replicates identical profiles every region.

title: UTOPIA input profiles (deterministic) — utopia_profiles
text: list tidy data.frames, replicated across regions: weather (resource, region, slice, wval), demand (region, slice, load – relative load shape) stock (region, tech, gw – base-year capacity).

title: Validate interpolated scenario parameters — validate_scenario_parameters
text: Runs set post-interpolation consistency checks numeric / bounds / map parameters scenario reports issues. Checks: NA index columns: NA parameter's dimSets id columns. fold = TRUE, NA permitted trimmable dimensions (region / slice / vintage, fold encodes wildcards); fold = FALSE, NA permitted id column. Schema: data columns match declared dimSets (plus value, type bounds). Duplicate keys: duplicate id tuples. Map vs parameter (correctness): every tuple value map covered source parameter; missing value otherwise silently replaced solver default. Parameter vs map (efficiency): value parameter rows lie within union maps (orphan / --domain rows). trimming must hold exactly.

Use distingush different structures subsets time-slices. name used propose default folder names model/scenario scripts separate solutions scenario different calendar objects. desc character. Description calendar object, references. timeframes list. Named list nested sub-annual levels vectors individual elements. top level list highest level calendar, e.g., "ANNUAL". lowest level smallest time-slice, e.g., "MONTH". "ANNUAL" default (hardwired) top level calendar. levels optional, create nested sub-annual levels time-slices. minimum number time-slices timeframe two (except top level). year_fraction numeric. fraction year covered calendar, e.g. 1 annual calendar (default), 0.5 semi-annual, 0.25 quarterly, etc. Currently must specified manually subset calendars validate sum shares. timetable data.frame. Data frame levels timeframes named columns, number rows equal total number time-slices lowest level. Every timeframe set time-slices ("slices") - named fragment time year-share. Timeframes nested structure every slice serves parent lower level time-slices (children). first column name time-slice, rest columns names timeframes. values share year covered time-slice. sum shares every timeframe equal 1. weight optional column weight time-slice year, used sumpled/subset selection time-slices. slice_share data.frame. Auto-calculated timetable two column data.frame slices levels individual share year. first column name time-slice, second column share year covered time-slice. default_timeframe character. name default level time-slices used model. specified, lowest level timeframes used default timeframe. timeframe_rank character. Auto-calculated timetable timeframes slots named character vector ranks timeframes. rank used determine order timeframes calendar. slices_in_frame integer. Auto-calculated timetable Number time-slices every timeframe. slice_family data.frame. Auto-calculated timetable data.frame mapping "parent" "child" slices two nearest timeframes nested hierarchy. first column name parent time-slice, second column name child time-slice. slice_ancestry data.frame. Auto-calculated timetable data.frame mapping "child", "grandchild", etc. slices "parent"  "grandparent" time-slices full hierarchy. first column name (grand-) child time-slice, second column name (grand-) parent time-slice. next_in_timeframe data.frame. Auto-calculated timetable data.frame mapping chronological sequence time-slices timeframe. first column name time-slice, second column name next time-slice timeframe. next_in_year data.frame. Auto-calculated timetable data.frame mapping chronological sequence time-slices timeframe whole year. first column name time-slice, second column name next time-slice timeframe. misc list. additional data information store object.

title: Contributing to energyRt
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("optimal2050/energyRt", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to energyRt
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use styler package apply styles, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Oleg Lugovoy. Author, maintainer. Vladimir Potashnikov. Author. Tarun Sharma. Contributor.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Lugovoy O, Potashnikov V (2026). energyRt: Energy Systems Modeling Toolbox. R package version 0.70.0.9000, https://energyRt.org.
code: @Manual{,   title = {energyRt: Energy Systems Modeling Toolbox},   author = {Oleg Lugovoy and Vladimir Potashnikov},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.70.0.9000},   url = {https://energyRt.org}, }

title: Energy Systems Modeling Toolbox
text: energyRt (energy system modeling R-toolbox /ˈɛnərdʒi ɑrt/) macro-language energy system modeling R. describe energy system — fuels, technologies, resources demands — R objects; energyRt compiles full capacity-expansion & dispatch optimization model, solves , returns tidy results ready dplyr/ggplot2 analysis. technical layer (sets, equations, solver files, results parsing) generated , concentrate system modeling, code optimizes .

title: Energy Systems Modeling Toolbox
text: energyRt optimization model (~100 predefined equations, extendable newConstraint()) implemented four mathematical-programming languages. model object solves , consistent results: Start zero-setup GLPK; switch backends later without touching model.

title: Energy Systems Modeling Toolbox
text: complete model — one fuel, one power plant, one demand — built, solved read ~20 lines (GLPK, external setup needed Windows Rtools): Get started vignette walks example ideas behind .
code: library(energyRt)  GAS <- newCommodity("GAS", timeframe = "ANNUAL") ELC <- newCommodity("ELC", timeframe = "ANNUAL")  SUP_GAS <- newSupply("SUP_GAS", commodity = "GAS",                      availability = data.frame(cost = 6.0))   # fuel price, MEUR/PJ  EGAS <- newTechnology("EGAS",   input   = list(comm = "GAS"), output = list(comm = "ELC"),   ceff    = data.frame(comm = "GAS", cinp2use = 0.55),        # 55% efficient   invcost = list(invcost = 900),                              # MEUR/GW   fixom   = 25, cap2act = 31.536, olife = 25L)  DEM_ELC <- newDemand("DEM_ELC", commodity = "ELC",                      dem = data.frame(dem = 50))               # 50 PJ a year  mod <- newModel("HELLO",   data    = newRepository("parts", GAS, ELC, SUP_GAS, EGAS, DEM_ELC),   region  = "R1", discount = 0.05,   horizon = newHorizon(period = 2025:2040, intervals = c(1, 5, 10),                        mid_is_end = TRUE))  scen <- solve_scenario(interpolate_model(mod, name = "BASE"),                        solver = solver_options$glpk)  getData(scen, "vObjective", merge = TRUE)   # total discounted system cost getData(scen, "vTechCap",   merge = TRUE)   # capacity the model built, GW

title: Energy Systems Modeling Toolbox
text: Model bricks — commodities, technologies (efficiencies, fuel blends & shares, auxiliary flows like emissions cooling water), supply, demand, storage, weather, trade; draw() sketches technology diagram. → Model bricks UTOPIA teaching model — complete multi-region electricity model built step step, shipped utopia_modules data kit ready scenario levers (CO₂ cap, carbon tax, renewable share, nuclear moratorium). → UTOPIA : building model · UTOPIA II: running scenarios Levelized cost — levcost() prices technology -priori (screening, textbook LCOE) ex-post solved scenario, autoplot() cost breakdowns. Reports — report() renders HTML/PDF datasheet technology solved process, levcost included. Scenario workflow — tidy result extraction getData(), scenario editing & re-solving, Arrow-backed save_scenario()/load_scenario() larger--memory results. → Workflow · Plotting

title: Energy Systems Modeling Toolbox
text: Get started — core idea ten minutes. Tutorials — installation, solver backends, model bricks, UTOPIA, workflow, plotting. useR! workshop — hands-training course built energyRt UTOPIA model (Quarto book, preparation). IDEEA — open multi-region model India’s power system, built energyRt: production-scale application.

title: Energy Systems Modeling Toolbox
text: reproduce pre-2026 models, install frozen legacy release instead: pak::pkg_install("optimal2050/energyRt@v0.50"). need least one solver backend. Windows, GLPK ships Rtools — extra setup. Julia/JuMP, Python/Pyomo, GAMS see installation article solver backends article.
code: pak::pkg_install("optimal2050/energyRt") # or remotes::install_github("optimal2050/energyRt")

title: Energy Systems Modeling Toolbox
text: current development line (v0.60.x) modernizes interpolation pipeline, scenario storage, analysis tools (levcost, report, autoplot) way v1.0. v0.50 release (“half-way-”) frozen remains available pre-2026 modeling projects; model code, classes methods receive fixes . package website: https://energyrt.org

title: energyRt 0.50.9-dev
text: New features & critical changes model code: Weighting time-slices subset revised rewritten. Since version, variables ‘slice’ dimension weighed consistency slice-level operation across sampled non-sampled model runs. Slice-weights can vary across model years. variables ‘year’ dimension weighted interval lengths.  Exception: cumulative variables (vSupReserveCum, etc.) account interval length every milestone year.  capacity variables (vTechCap, etc.) represent state variable end period, including accumulation retirement capacity interval milestone year. New capacity variables (vTechNewCap, etc.) given year. Period length (pPeriodLen) must applied annual capacity additions (vTechNewCap) get total new capacity process end period. System costs regrouped type (capital, fixed O&M, variable O&M, supply, taxes, subsidies) process type (technology, trade, storage, etc.) facilitate analysis cost structure. Total Costs equation rewritten reflect new cost structure.Bug fixes Early retirement option (optimizeRetirment = TRUE) corrected exclude retirement “new” technologies time installation. draw() method ‘trade’ fixed exclude repeated arrows plot. newCosts() debugged, example added Utopia tutorial. tsl2hour() fixed able identify n-digits hours (previously worked 2 ).Miscellaneous new version (3) logo design idea (DALL-E). code clean-, testing, documentation progress comply CRAN requirements. Functions/method progress: levcost() report(), add_weights add_intervals arguments added getData() function add time-slice weights interval lengths requested data applicable.

title: energyRt 0.50.7-dev
text: Fixed stability issues draw() method. Added “Hello World” example tutorial. new version logo design idea (DALL-E). code clean-documentation progress comply CRAN requirements. first draft package CRAN-like manual added. version might unstable due ongoing changes.

title: energyRt 0.50.6-dev
text: draw() drafted processes: ‘technology’, ‘export’, ‘import’, ‘supply’, ‘demand’, ‘trade’, ’storage docs completed main classes examples. code clean-documentation progress.

title: energyRt 0.50.5-dev
text: draw() rewritten based ‘grid’ package, now generic method. added draw() methods ‘technology’, ‘export’, ‘import’ classes. fixed several interface-level bugs introduced 0.50.4-dev clean-documentation.

title: energyRt 0.50.4-dev
text: Documentation classes progress (~70% docs completed). Logo-search started! “logo” page added. Website reshaped, added new, populated yet “articles”. !!! Tested!!! Due ongoing changes documentation functions/methods clean-, version may “surprises” - tests progress.

title: energyRt 0.50.3-dev
text: Development version preparation CRAN submission. Added NEWS.md file track changes package. Added functions document classes yaml file ‘classes.yaml’. technology-class newTechnology function documented.
